name: Chern-Simons Stiffness Pump (anyon statistical rigidity)
axis: exotic quasiparticle statistics — anyons as a STIFFNESS route
basis: REAL: anyon superconductivity (Laughlin 1988; Chen-Wilczek-Witten-Halperin 1989). Critically, arXiv:2506.02108 (2025, 'Anyon Superconductivity and Plateau Transitions in Doped Fractional Quantum Anomalous Hall Insulators', PRL 10.1103/6bgj-bfdn) derives an ANOMALOUSLY LARGE superfluid stiffness near a Chern-Simons QED3 transition, where the stiffness is set by composite-fermion polarizability rather than by a bosonic-glue energy scale. The statistical (Chern-Simons) gauge field, not a phonon/magnon, supplies the phase rigidity.
falsifiable_prediction: In a doped FQAH/anyon model, the computed superfluid stiffness D_s scales with the Chern-Simons level k (statistical angle theta = pi/k) and the composite-fermion compressibility, NOT with any boson energy omega. PREDICTION: there exists a filling/level window where D_s exceeds the spin-fluctuation ceiling-equivalent (k_B*164K) at fixed carrier density, with D_s diverging as the system approaches the IQH plateau transition. FALSIFIED if D_s collapses back to the Uemura/boson-limited line for all k.
beats_phase_stiffness_ceiling: The 134-164K Emery-Kivelson wall is a BOSON-GLUE phase-stiffness ceiling: D_s is bounded by the glue energy scale times carrier density. Anyon statistics removes the glue entirely — the phase rigidity comes from the statistical gauge field's response (composite-fermion polarizability), a quantity with no 200meV phonon cap and no 164K spin-fluctuation cap. The stiffness is geometric/topological in origin, decoupling D_s from the fluctuation energy that traps boson-glued condensates.
novelty: Orthogonal to all 5 boson-glue families (no phonon/exciton/plasmon/magnon glue at all — the 'glue' is statistics) and orthogonal to the closed-negative H_012 topology-gap lever, which bought only pairing AMPLITUDE and collapsed to geometry. This attacks STIFFNESS directly via gauge-field response, the exact quantity H_012 proved topology could NOT supply. Distinct from the flat-band quantum-geometry lever (that is band tr-g; this is many-body Chern-Simons response).

name: Z_N Parafermion Charge-Ne Condensate (high-rigidity multi-charge order)
axis: exotic quasiparticle statistics — parafermions / charge-Ne pairing as a STIFFNESS route
basis: REAL: charge-4e superconductivity with parafermionic vortices (researchgate 400584125; arXiv:2510.26686 FQAH-SC heterostructures; Z_3 Read-Rezayi -> charge-2e correspondence). Z_{2m} parafermion symmetry lets a Cooper pair split into 2m indistinguishable parts (proximitized nu=1/m FQH edge). A charge-Ne condensate has a stiffness that scales with the SQUARE of the condensing charge: D_s ~ (Ne)^2 * n_pair / m*, so going from 2e to 4e or 6e parafermionic order multiplies the phase rigidity at fixed pair density.
falsifiable_prediction: In a parafermion/charge-Ne mean-field model, computed D_s scales as (Nq)^2 where q=e. PREDICTION: a charge-4e parafermion condensate reaches the same D_s as a charge-2e BCS condensate at 1/4 the carrier density (or 4x the stiffness at equal density), pushing the BKT-equivalent T onto/above the 164K line. FALSIFIED if the parafermion fractionalization simultaneously suppresses n_pair by >=(N/2)^2, exactly canceling the (Ne)^2 gain (the honest null).
beats_phase_stiffness_ceiling: The Emery-Kivelson ceiling assumes charge-2e Cooper pairs glued by a fluctuation with capped energy. A charge-Ne parafermion condensate breaks the assumption at the prefactor level: D_s carries an (Ne)^2 enhancement independent of the glue energy omega. If parafermionic correlations stiffen the phase without paying a proportional density penalty, the BKT T = (pi/2)D_s can clear 164K even with a sub-200meV underlying scale — the ceiling is per-charge-2e, not fundamental.
novelty: Charge-Ne (N>2) order is categorically outside the charge-2e boson-glue framework that defined all 5 surveyed glue families and the Emery-Kivelson wall. Orthogonal to flat-band geometry (single-particle) and to multilayer-D_s boost (that stacks 2e layers; this raises the per-condensate charge). Not the closed topology-gap lever — parafermions here are a stiffness/order-parameter multiplicity lever, not a protected gap.

name: Statistical-Transmutation Fluctuation Trap-Breaker
axis: exotic quasiparticle statistics — flux attachment to defeat the order-traps law
basis: REAL: flux-attachment / statistical transmutation (Wilczek; Jain composite fermions; Fradkin). Attaching flux quanta converts a bosonic order parameter's phase-winding cost. SPECULATIVE application: the campaign's deep law 'the fluctuation glues, the order traps' says any condensate that orders gets trapped by a competing order (CDW/SDW) at the same energy scale. Flux attachment changes the BRAIDING phase of the competing order's defects, potentially raising the trap order's free energy relative to the SC channel without weakening the glue.
falsifiable_prediction: In a model with co-existing SC and CDW/SDW channels plus a Chern-Simons flux-attachment term, PREDICTION: tuning the statistical angle theta shifts the SC-vs-trap-order free-energy crossing so that for a theta-window the SC channel wins where it lost at theta=0 (bosonic), and the SC phase-ordering T in that window exceeds the theta=0 trap-limited ceiling. FALSIFIED if flux attachment raises the SC channel and the trap channel by equal amounts (no relative gain) across all theta.
beats_phase_stiffness_ceiling: The 134-164K wall is the energy at which spin fluctuations both glue AND trap (order traps). Statistical transmutation acts on the COMPETITION, not the glue: by changing defect braiding statistics it can make the trap order's condensation more costly while leaving (or enhancing) the SC fluctuation glue, breaking the glue=trap degeneracy that pins the ceiling. It attacks the 'order traps' half of the deep law rather than the 'fluctuation glues' half.
novelty: Directly targets the campaign's META-LAW (fluctuation glues/order traps) rather than a single glue family — orthogonal to every glue-channel seed because it manipulates the competing-order side. No surveyed lever (flat-band, 3D, multilayer, driven, topology-gap) touches the SC-vs-trap free-energy crossing via statistics. Distinct from H_012 (gap) and from driven/Floquet (transient): this is an equilibrium statistical-angle lever.

name: Non-Abelian Vortex Entanglement Rigidity (braiding-protected phase coherence)
axis: exotic quasiparticle statistics — non-abelian anyons / Majorana vortices as a stiffness route
basis: REAL: non-abelian Ising anyons / Majorana zero modes in vortices of p+ip / topological superconductors (Moore-Read; Read-Green; Ivanov braiding). SPECULATIVE extension: in a 2D SC the phase-coherence-killing excitations are free vortices (BKT). If vortices bind non-abelian zero modes, vortex pairs share a non-local topological degeneracy; the resulting non-local entanglement could add a topological contribution to the vortex-unbinding free energy, raising the effective vortex core energy / fugacity cost and thus the BKT temperature.
falsifiable_prediction: In a 2D topological SC model with Majorana-bearing vortices, PREDICTION: the BKT transition temperature computed WITH the non-local fermion-parity degeneracy of vortex pairs included is higher than the bare-stiffness BKT estimate (pi/2)D_s, because the topological vortex-pair binding adds to the unbinding cost. FALSIFIED if the non-local degeneracy contributes zero (or entropic, T-lowering) free energy to vortex unbinding — i.e., braiding statistics is irrelevant to the BKT energy balance (the likely honest null).
beats_phase_stiffness_ceiling: The Emery-Kivelson ceiling is a BARE-stiffness BKT bound: T_BKT = (pi/2)D_s with D_s glue-limited. This route adds a SECOND, topological term to the vortex-unbinding free energy that is NOT set by D_s or by glue energy — it is set by the non-abelian fusion/braiding structure. If positive, T_BKT can exceed (pi/2)D_s even when D_s itself is stuck at the 164K-equivalent value, defeating the ceiling by renormalizing vortex energetics rather than stiffness.
novelty: Attacks T_BKT through VORTEX energetics (the unbinding cost), a channel none of the surveyed levers touch — all glue families and the geometry/3D/multilayer levers raise D_s, never the vortex core/binding term. Uses non-abelian statistics for COHERENCE not computation, orthogonal to the standard topological-quantum-computing framing and to the closed H_012 gap lever (Majorana there protected a gap; here they protect phase coherence).

name: Bethe-Ceiling Lattice (negative-curvature stiffness ladder)
axis: synthetic & extra dimensions — hyperbolic / curved lattices raising superfluid stiffness
basis: GROUNDED concept, SPECULATIVE application. The phase-stiffness BKT ceiling is a strictly-2D pathology: the Mermin-Wagner/BKT logarithmic vortex unbinding that pins D_s-limited T_c only happens because the 2D plane has marginal connectivity (effective coordination z that grows polynomially with system size). On a hyperbolic (negatively-curved) lattice the number of sites within radius R grows EXPONENTIALLY, so the lattice is locally finite-D but has an effective spectral dimension > 2 and a non-zero percolation/spin-stiffness threshold — there is NO BKT phase: long-range order survives at finite T even with a continuous symmetry (the Mermin-Wagner theorem fails on hyperbolic graphs; cf. circle-packing / hyperbolic-lattice physics, Kollar-Houck-et-al hyperbolic circuit-QED lattices, Nature 571, 45 (2019); hyperbolic band theory, Maciejko-Rayan PNAS 2022). Emery-Kivelson's phase-stiffness cap assumes a 2D superfluid; a superconductor whose Cooper-pair phase field lives on a hyperbolic connectivity graph would have its phase-disordering temperature set by a mean-field-like (Ising-stiffness) scale, not the BKT scale.
falsifiable_prediction: Solve the XY model (Cooper-pair phase field) on a {7,3} or {5,4} hyperbolic tiling at fixed bare stiffness J equal to the cuprate value (~the J that gives BKT T_c = 134 K on a square lattice). Prediction: the hyperbolic-lattice phase-ordering T_c exceeds the square-lattice BKT T_c by a factor >= 1.7 (i.e. crosses 230 K) at the SAME microscopic J, because vortex entropy can no longer compensate vortex energy on an exponentially-growing graph. FALSIFIED if hyperbolic-lattice Monte-Carlo T_c(J) <= 1.2x the square-lattice BKT T_c(J).
beats_phase_stiffness_ceiling: The 134-164 K wall is a BKT (Emery-Kivelson 2D phase-fluctuation) wall, not a pairing-energy wall. Negative curvature destroys the BKT mechanism itself: vortices cost energy growing with graph size while their entropy stays bounded, so the Kosterlitz-Thouless free-energy crossing (which sets T_c ~ pi*D_s/2) is replaced by a mean-field crossing T_c ~ z*J that is parametrically higher for the SAME D_s. It does not raise the pairing glue; it removes the phase-fluctuation penalty that the surveyed ceiling is made of.
novelty: Surveyed work treats stiffness via the quantum-metric D_s bound (raise D_s) and via 2D->3D dimension COUNT (1.84x). This is orthogonal: it keeps D_s fixed and changes the CONNECTIVITY GEOMETRY of the phase manifold so the BKT formula no longer applies. The F2/F9 forks named 'synthetic dimension' and 'Unruh curved frame' abstractly but never identified Mermin-Wagner-failure-on-hyperbolic-graphs as the specific stiffness escape.

name: Moire-of-Moire Stiffness Cascade (hierarchical-scale phase rigidity)
axis: synthetic & extra dimensions — moire-of-moire raising superfluid stiffness
basis: GROUNDED substrate, SPECULATIVE mechanism. Moire-of-moire (a second moire formed between two moire superlattices, supermoire / quasi-periodic moire) creates a HIERARCHY of length scales L1 << L2, each with its own flat band and its own local stiffness. Real: supermoire in twisted-trilayer and helical graphene has been imaged (e.g. helical trilayer graphene, Nature 2024; double-moire / supermoire band reconstruction). Speculative leap: superfluid stiffness in a single moire flat band is small BECAUSE the band is flat (D_s ~ quantum-metric-limited, the surveyed ceiling). But a moire-of-moire stacks a SLOW phase-stiffness channel on top of the fast one: phase coherence on the long L2 scale is mediated by a renormalized stiffness that is the harmonic/series combination of the two scales, and the LONGER-wavelength phase mode is stiffer against thermal vortex unbinding because its vortex cores are L2-sized (huge core energy). The vortex-core energy E_c ~ D_s*ln(L2/a) grows with the supermoire period, pushing the BKT temperature up.
falsifiable_prediction: Build a two-scale XY/Lawrence-Doniach model with a fast moire stiffness D1 and a slow supermoire stiffness D2 (D2 set by interlayer Josephson coupling between moire domains). Prediction: BKT T_c scales as ~D1*[1 + alpha*ln(L2/L1)] with alpha > 0, so increasing the supermoire-to-moire period ratio L2/L1 from 1 to ~10 raises T_c by >= 30% at fixed bare D1. FALSIFIED if the two-scale BKT T_c is independent of (or decreases with) L2/L1 in the Monte-Carlo, i.e. the supermoire adds no stiffness.
beats_phase_stiffness_ceiling: The Emery-Kivelson cap T_c ~ pi*D_s/2 uses a SINGLE-scale stiffness. A moire-of-moire gives a logarithmically vortex-core-enhanced effective stiffness because the smallest stable vortex must be supermoire-sized, multiplying the core energy in the KT free-energy balance. It beats the wall not by more glue but by raising the vortex-unbinding cost via a built-in long length scale — a lever absent from the single-flat-band D_s analysis.
novelty: The surveyed multilayer-D_s-boost and CoSn flat-band geometry are all SINGLE-scale. Moire-of-moire is a genuinely different object: a stiffness CASCADE / hierarchy where the vortex-core length is a free design parameter. Orthogonal to 'raise the integral tr g' (single band) and to 'add a 3rd dimension'.

name: Synthetic-Dimension Coordination Pump (extra hopping axis without extra space)
axis: synthetic & extra dimensions — synthetic-dimension lattices raising superfluid stiffness
basis: GROUNDED platform, SPECULATIVE for SC. Synthetic dimensions encode an extra lattice axis in an internal degree of freedom (atomic hyperfine states, photonic frequency/OAM modes, or orbital/sublattice index) with engineered hopping along it (Boada et al. PRL 2012; Celi et al. PRL 2014; Ozawa-Price Nature Rev Phys 2019). Speculative leap: phase stiffness D_s is, in a tight-binding picture, set by the pair-hopping bandwidth and the COORDINATION NUMBER z (number of Josephson-coupled neighbors). A synthetic dimension adds neighbors in an internal index WITHOUT diluting the real-space pairing or adding real-space kinetic energy that would broaden the flat band. So one can raise z (hence D_s ~ z*t_pair) while keeping the real-space band flat for the geometric pairing enhancement — decoupling 'flat band for pairing' from 'high coordination for stiffness', which are forced to trade off in real space.
falsifiable_prediction: In an attractive-Hubbard / pair-stiffness calculation, attach an N-rung synthetic dimension (internal index with uniform synthetic hopping t_s) to a 2D flat band and compute D_s vs N. Prediction: D_s grows monotonically with synthetic coordination, D_s(N) ~ D_s(1)*(1 + c*(N-1)) with c > 0, while the real-space band flatness (hence the quantum-metric pairing) is unchanged — so the phase-stiffness T_c rises past 164 K for N >= ~3 synthetic rungs at fixed pairing. FALSIFIED if D_s saturates or is pinned by the minimal-quantum-metric bound regardless of N (i.e. synthetic neighbors do not contribute to physical phase rigidity).
beats_phase_stiffness_ceiling: The ceiling exists because in REAL space, the flat band needed for strong pairing also kills the kinetic stiffness (flat = heavy = low D_s). A synthetic dimension supplies stiffness-giving coordination through an internal index that is NOT subject to the real-space flatness constraint, breaking the pairing-vs-stiffness trade-off that the spin-fluctuation/flat-band families both hit. T_c ~ pi*D_s/2 rises because D_s rises while pairing is held.
novelty: All surveyed flat-band work lives in real-space 2D/3D and is bounded by D_s >= |C| / D_s ∝ minimal quantum metric for ONE band. The synthetic axis is not a counted real dimension (orthogonal to the 1.84x 3D lever) and not Floquet (it is static engineered hopping). The specific claim — synthetic coordination decouples pairing-flatness from stiffness-coordination — is absent from the survey.

name: Aperiodic / Quasicrystal Vortex Localizer (no Goldstone soft mode to disorder phase)
axis: synthetic & extra dimensions — extra-dimensional projection (quasicrystals as cuts of higher-D lattices) raising superfluid stiffness
basis: GROUNDED, SPECULATIVE for the stiffness mechanism. A quasicrystal is literally a projection (cut-and-project) of a periodic lattice in a HIGHER dimension (e.g. 2D Penrose = cut of 5D cubic lattice) — a real extra-dimensional construction. Quasicrystals support superconductivity (Kamiya et al., Al-Zn-Mg quasicrystal SC, Nature Comm 2018; theoretical flat-band-like states and multifractal wavefunctions in quasiperiodic SC, Sakai-Arita). Speculative leap: in a quasicrystal there is no Brillouin zone and no sharply-defined long-wavelength Goldstone phason-free phase mode of the usual BKT type; multifractal critical eigenstates pin vortices (vortex glass with high depinning energy) and the absence of a clean q->0 phase mode raises the effective phase-disordering temperature. The phase stiffness is set by the higher-D periodic parent's connectivity, projected down.
falsifiable_prediction: Compute the superfluid stiffness and BKT/vortex-unbinding temperature for an attractive-Hubbard model on a Penrose (or Ammann-Beenker) tiling vs a periodic lattice of equal mean coordination and equal pairing. Prediction: the quasicrystal's phase-coherence T_c exceeds the periodic reference by >= 20% at matched D_s because multifractal states raise the vortex-core pinning energy and suppress the soft q->0 phase mode. FALSIFIED if the quasicrystal T_c is <= the periodic reference (i.e. quasiperiodicity gives no stiffness/pinning advantage), or if multifractality instead suppresses D_s.
beats_phase_stiffness_ceiling: Emery-Kivelson assumes a translation-invariant 2D superfluid with a soft Goldstone phase mode that thermally unbinds vortices at T_c ~ pi*D_s/2. A quasicrystal (higher-D projection) lacks that clean soft mode and pins vortices via multifractal localization, so the free vortices that destroy phase order are gapped/pinned and the disordering temperature rises above the periodic-lattice BKT value at the same D_s.
novelty: Orthogonal to every surveyed family: not phonon/exciton/plasmon/magnon glue, not the single-band quantum-metric D_s boost, not the counted 3D lever, not Floquet, not topology-as-independent-lever. The lever is APERIODIC ORDER (a higher-D projection) acting on the phase-fluctuation sector via vortex pinning — a mechanism the campaign has not enumerated.

name: ETA-LOCK — single-seed Liouvillian dark-state pump (autonomous η-pairing NESS)
axis: STEADY-STATE non-equilibrium — continuously-driven open-system SC; dissipative/reservoir-engineered pairing
basis: GROUNDED-but-speculative-for-RTSC. η-pairing ODLRO is a real exact-eigenstate phenomenon (Yang, Phys. Rev. Lett. 63, 2144 (1989)) in which on-site Cooper pairs carry off-diagonal long-range order that is DISTANCE-INDEPENDENT (protected by the Hubbard SU(2) pseudospin/SO(4) symmetry — η correlations do not decay with separation). The autonomous-stabilization mechanism is real and recent: a Lindblad evolution with a single LOCAL rotated η-pair lowering jump operator pumps the lattice from vacuum into a NESS with macroscopic η-pair ODLRO — one local dissipative seed synchronizes the whole lattice ('Single-site dissipation stabilizes a superconducting nonequilibrium steady state in a strongly correlated system', arXiv:2602.00452; heating-route precursor: Kaneko/Shirakawa/Yunoki, 'Heating-Induced Long-Range η-Pairing in the Hubbard Model', PRL 123, 030602 (2019), arXiv:1901.xxxx). SPECULATIVE extension = pushing this NESS to a real material near 293 K.
falsifiable_prediction: In an in-silico Hubbard/extended-Hubbard lattice driven by a single local rotated-η Lindblad jump operator, the steady-state η-correlation C(r)=<η†_i η_j> converges to a NONZERO, distance-INDEPENDENT plateau whose magnitude is set by U/t and the dissipation rate γ but is INDEPENDENT of system temperature entering through the spin bath — i.e. the ODLRO plateau survives to spin-bath temperatures >> 164 K with NO Kosterlitz-Thouless-like stiffness collapse. Falsified if the plateau decays with r (true ODLRO absent), or if it collapses at a stiffness-scaling temperature matching the Emery-Kivelson wall.
beats_phase_stiffness_ceiling: The Emery-Kivelson ceiling (~134-164K) is a PHASE-STIFFNESS wall: it caps T_c via the superfluid stiffness of a finite-coherence-length condensate (KT/3D-XY argument). η-pairing ODLRO is on-site (zero coherence length) and distance-INDEPENDENT, so there is no phase-gradient energy and no stiffness to thermally unbind — the order is enforced by the Liouvillian DARK STATE / SU(2) pseudospin symmetry, not by thermodynamic rigidity. Replacing 'stiffness rigidity' with 'symmetry/dynamical rigidity' removes the very quantity the ceiling bounds.
novelty: Orthogonal to all 5 glue families (no boson mediates — pairing is an exact-eigenstate symmetry, not glue, so 'the fluctuation glues, the order traps' law does not apply: there is no competing fluctuation). Orthogonal to flat-band/3D/multilayer (geometry-agnostic). Orthogonal to the closed driven/Floquet ruling (that was TRANSIENT ps-ns photo-SC; this is a time-independent-Hamiltonian AUTONOMOUS NESS — no pulsed laser, the dissipator is a coupling, not a power pulse). Orthogonal to the brainstorm's F10/O4 (those named NESS/dissipation-engineering generically and closed them as 'reduces to BORROW power bill'; this seed names the SPECIFIC distance-independent-ODLRO escape that makes the power bill irrelevant to T_c).

name: STIFFNESS-FREE TIME CRYSTAL — boundary-time-crystal rigidity as the order-protector
basis: GROUNDED concept, SPECULATIVE for SC. Boundary/continuous time crystals (BTC) are real open-system NESS phases that spontaneously break CONTINUOUS time-translation symmetry under a time-INDEPENDENT Lindbladian, sustained by collective (long-range) dissipation — not by periodic drive (Iemini et al., 'Boundary Time Crystals', PRL 121, 035301 (2018); exact-solution & rigidity: Carollo & Lesanovsky, Phys. Rev. A 105, L040202 (2022), arXiv:2110.00030; solid-state rigidity/criticality: 'Criticality and rigidity of dissipative discrete time crystals in solids', Phys. Rev. Research 4, 023025 (2022)). The order parameter persists as a macroscopic limit-cycle oscillation with its OWN rigidity (robust to perturbation). SPECULATIVE = casting the SC order parameter Δ(t) as the oscillating BTC observable.
falsifiable_prediction: In an in-silico collectively-dissipating pairing model (long-range-coupled fermion sites + collective Lindblad loss/gain), the pair amplitude Δ(t) enters a self-sustained limit cycle whose oscillation AMPLITUDE (the rigid order parameter) is set by the gain/loss ratio and is RIGID against on-site dephasing up to a dephasing rate that scales with the collective coupling N·g, NOT with the single-site stiffness. Falsified if Δ(t) rigidity scales as the equilibrium superfluid stiffness (collapses at ~164 K-equivalent), or if no symmetry-broken limit cycle forms (decays to a static fixed point).
beats_phase_stiffness_ceiling: A BTC's rigidity is a DYNAMICAL rigidity from balanced collective gain/loss and long-range interaction (the limit cycle resists perturbation collectively, ∝N), categorically different from the static superfluid phase stiffness the Emery-Kivelson bound caps. The ceiling bounds the THERMAL unbinding of a static phase; a time-crystal order parameter is never static — it is protected by the spectral structure of the Liouvillian (purely-imaginary eigenvalue pair), which has no temperature in the usual stiffness sense. The wall's governing quantity (static ρ_s) is simply not the protector here.
novelty: Distinct from the closed Floquet/driven ruling: discrete time crystals need a periodic DRIVE (transient/power-bound, already closed); a BOUNDARY/continuous time crystal needs only a static Lindbladian — the symmetry broken is CONTINUOUS time-translation, a different object. Orthogonal to all glue families (the protector is a limit-cycle spectral gap, not a mediating boson). The brainstorm's A8 ('time-crystal subharmonic ladder') was a DRIVEN subharmonic-tone idea on the BORROW reservoir axis; this seed is the UNDRIVEN boundary-TC whose novelty is order-parameter rigidity, not energy borrowing.

name: DARK-STATE PAIR PUMP — dissipation-induced topological SC steady state (loss-shaped attractor)
axis: STEADY-STATE non-equilibrium — reservoir/dissipation-engineered pairing as a Liouvillian attractor (dark state)
basis: GROUNDED mechanism class, SPECULATIVE for room-T. Driven-dissipative state preparation: engineer the jump operators so the TARGET paired/topological state is the unique Liouvillian DARK STATE (annihilated by all jump operators), making it the global attractor regardless of initial state (foundational: Diehl et al., 'Quantum states and phases in driven open quantum systems', Nat. Phys. 4, 878 (2008); Verstraete-Wolf-Cirac, Nat. Phys. 5, 633 (2009); SC-specific recent: 'Dissipation-Induced Steady States in Topological Superconductors: Mechanisms and Design Principles', arXiv:2508.11242; 'Dissipation engineering of fermionic long-range order beyond the Lindblad limit', Phys. Rev. B (p8n2-jzgh)). SPECULATIVE = engineering such a dark state in a solid-state electron system near 293 K.
falsifiable_prediction: Construct an in-silico jump-operator set whose unique dark state has finite pair ODLRO; the NESS pair correlation should be set ENTIRELY by the engineered dissipation gap (Liouvillian gap Δ_L) and be INSENSITIVE to the equilibrium pairing scale T_c0 — specifically, the steady-state condensate fraction is a function of γ/Δ_L only, with no thermal cutoff at the stiffness wall as long as Δ_L exceeds the thermal-fluctuation injection rate. Falsified if the dark state is non-unique (degenerate attractor → no robust order), or if NESS order tracks the equilibrium T_c (no dissipative enhancement).
beats_phase_stiffness_ceiling: The order is the attractor of a Liouvillian whose dark-state subspace is protected by the LIOUVILLIAN GAP Δ_L (relaxation gap to the steady state), not by free-energy stiffness. Thermal fluctuations are a competing jump process injected at rate ∝T; order survives whenever Δ_L > injection rate — a condition on engineered dissipation, NOT on the material's superfluid stiffness. You beat the ~164 K wall by making Δ_L large by DESIGN (loss-shaping) rather than by raising ρ_s. The temperature only enters as a noise rate to be out-competed, decoupling T_c from the stiffness bound.
novelty: Orthogonal to the campaign's 'order traps' law: here the dissipation is ENGINEERED so that the SC order IS the dark-state attractor — competing order is loss-shaped AWAY (made a non-dark, decaying mode) rather than competing with SC. This inverts the trap. Distinct from F10/O4 brainstorm closures: those argued NESS/dissipation 'reduces to the BORROW power bill'; this seed's escape is that the relevant comparison is Δ_L vs. thermal NOISE rate (a ratio), not total power vs. condensation energy — a maintenance power that can be small if Δ_L is engineered large, so the bill need not scale with T_c. Orthogonal to all boson-glue and geometry levers.

name: Vortex-Code Phase Lock (the superfluid phase as a protected logical variable)
basis: GROUNDED-anchor / SPECULATIVE-mechanism. The 134-164K ceiling is set by THERMAL VORTEX-ANTIVORTEX UNBINDING (Emery-Kivelson phase-stiffness / BKT), not by gap collapse — confirmed in the literature (Emery & Kivelson, Nature 374 (1995); BKT superfluid-stiffness universal jump). The seed: a free vortex is a logical error on the phase field, and a 2D toric/surface code is LITERALLY a Z2 gauge theory whose elementary excitations are point defects that an error-correcting Hamiltonian energetically confines. Couple the superconducting phase field to an auxiliary stabilizer lattice (engineered Josephson-junction / qubit array, cf. Kitaev's superconducting-array toric code, arXiv:cond-mat/0609441) whose stabilizers measure local vorticity and apply a confining string-tension term. The protection is on the DEFECT (vortex), not on the gap amplitude. SPECULATIVE that a static stabilizer term gives a thermodynamically real (not just per-cycle) vortex confinement in a continuum superfluid.
falsifiable_prediction: In a classical-XY / lattice-Josephson Monte-Carlo with an added local 'stabilizer' string-tension term S coupling to plaquette vorticity, the renormalized superfluid stiffness rho_s(T) will retain its universal-jump value up to T_BKT(S) = T_BKT(0) * (1 + alpha*S) with alpha>0 monotone, i.e. the BKT temperature rises continuously with stabilizer strength and does NOT saturate at the bare 134-164K-equivalent scale. Falsified if T_BKT is independent of S (confinement term renormalizes to zero) or if S merely shifts the prefactor without moving the vortex-unbinding point.
beats_phase_stiffness_ceiling: The 134-164K wall is the temperature at which vortices proliferate and unbind, destroying phase coherence at fixed bare stiffness. An error-correcting confinement term raises the vortex CORE ENERGY / string tension, pushing the unbinding temperature up WITHOUT needing more superfluid density or more glue — it attacks the loss channel (defect proliferation) directly rather than the gap. The gap/glue stays exactly where Emery-Kivelson left it; only the defect-entropy balance is changed.

name: POLARITON-BEC PROXIMITY: condensed-photon-fluid phase injection into an adjacent electron layer

name: Planckian Pair-Beat (Yukawa-SYK pairing of incoherent electrons)
basis: Esterlis & Schmalian, 'Cooper pairing of incoherent electrons', PRB 99, 214502 (2019) and Hauck/Esterlis–Schmalian Yukawa-SYK superconductivity: a 0+1D SYK-like boson Yukawa-coupled to fermions produces a marginal/non-Fermi-liquid normal state with NO quasiparticles, yet still has a finite, computable T_c. name: Overhauser-Locked Phase Ballast (nuclear-spin co-condensate)
basis: REAL building blocks: the contact hyperfine Hamiltonian A·I·S (Knight shift / Overhauser effect, Slichter, Principles of Magnetic Resonance) couples the electron spin/charge to a macroscopic nuclear-spin polarization. A spatially coherent Overhauser field is a genuine collective variable with its own stiffness (nuclear spin-wave / spin-diffusion modes, measured in GaAs DNP). No known SC uses the nuclear bath as a phase-rigidity ballast.

This seed does not touch the gap channel at all; it attacks the STIFFNESS term directly with a non-electronic reservoir — orthogonal to all five glue families and to the geometric/multilayer stiffness levers (which still source stiffness from the electron gas).

name: Hyperfine Two-Channel Suhl-Resonance (nuclear-moment Cooper bridge)
basis: REAL: the Suhl-Matthias-Walker two-band pairing framework (Phys. Rev. Lett. 3, 552 (1959)) shows an inter-channel coupling term can raise Tc above either channel alone; RKKY shows electrons can exchange a nuclear/local-moment-mediated interaction. SPECULATIVE: treat the nuclear-spin bath as a SECOND pairing channel whose 'boson' is a virtual nuclear-spin flip (frequency MHz-GHz, energy neV-ueV) so the retarded vertex is essentially instantaneous on electronic timescales — an anti-adiabatic, near-static attractive bridge between electrons sharing a nuclear site (analogous to but distinct from phonon, since omega_nuc << omega_Debye).

Prediction: the nuclear channel's contribution to Tc is set by the static nuclear susceptibility chi_nuc(T) ~ Curie 1/T, so the hyperfine pairing strength GROWS on cooling and must produce a positive curvature (upturn) in computed Tc vs hyperfine-coupling A that is absent for any finite-omega electronic boson. The Curie-law growth of chi_nuc means the effective coupling is strongest exactly where electronic glues are weakest (low T), so the ceiling mechanism (phase fluctuations melting a small-rho_s condensate) is partly sidestepped by a non-fluctuating, classical-moment bath.

The nuclear channel is uniquely ANTI-adiabatic AND its strength rises on cooling — neither property exists in the five families; it inverts the campaign's deep law because the nuclear bath does not order to trap.

name: Isotopic Knight-Shift Pairing Resonance (hyperfine Feshbach knob)
basis: REAL: Knight shift, nuclear quadrupole resonance, and the hyperfine constant A are isotope- and pressure-tunable; NMR Knight-shift collapse below Tc is THE classic singlet-pairing diagnostic (Yosida function). SPECULATIVE: run that diagnostic in reverse — use isotopic mass/moment substitution and quadrupolar level splitting as a Feshbach-like resonance knob to bring a nuclear-spin level into degeneracy with the electronic pairing energy, opening a hyperfine-resonant attractive channel only on resonance.

Prediction: sweeping B-field in an in-silico hyperfine-coupled BCS model yields a Tc(B) resonance at a field set by gamma_nuclear, distinguishable from any electronic Zeeman effect because the resonance field scales with the NUCLEAR g-factor (~2000x smaller), i.e. peak at kilo-Tesla-scale internal hyperfine fields, not lab fields. No nuclear-g-scaled resonance => falsified.

By only turning on at resonance it adds gap (and, via the locked nuclear polarization, phase ballast) without the competing-order/pseudogap penalty that caps spin-fluctuation glue, potentially lifting Tc at fixed rho_s^el.

A Feshbach-style nuclear resonance is a qualitatively different control axis (discrete nuclear levels, nuclear-g-scaled fields) absent from phonon/exciton/plasmon/magnon and from the geometry/topology levers.

name: Mossbauer-Lattice Recoilless Pair Channel (nuclear-recoil-free glue)
basis: REAL: the Mossbauer effect (recoilless nuclear gamma absorption, Lamb-Mossbauer factor) proves a nuclear transition can couple to the WHOLE lattice with zero recoil, and nuclear-resonant inelastic X-ray scattering measures nuclear-coupled phonon DOS. SPECULATIVE: a low-energy nuclear isomeric/quadrupole transition that is recoillessly coupled to the lattice could mediate an electron-electron interaction whose 'boson' is a recoil-free collective nuclear excitation — a glue with phonon-like lattice coherence but neV-keV nuclear energy and an essentially infinite (recoil-free) coherence length.
falsifiable_prediction: The pairing vertex should carry a Lamb-Mossbauer-factor weighting f_LM = exp(-k^2<x^2>): prediction is that the hyperfine pairing strength rises sharply as Debye-Waller suppression falls (stiffer lattice, lower T, heavier host), giving Tc a positive correlation with f_LM that is OPPOSITE to the ordinary isotope effect (Tc ~ M^-1/2). An in-silico scan over host stiffness must show Tc INCREASING with lattice rigidity if this channel dominates; a normal negative isotope exponent falsifies the recoilless channel.

novelty: Orthogonal to phonon glue (which IS recoiling/Debye-Waller-suppressed and capped at 200 meV) precisely because it is RECOIL-FREE: the sign of the isotope/stiffness dependence inverts. No surveyed family predicts Tc rising with lattice rigidity, and none invokes Mossbauer coherence as a stiffness source — distinct from the multilayer/3D geometric stiffness levers too.

name: Muonic Hydride Inversion (μ-H3S analog)
basis: GROUNDED scaffold, SPECULATIVE application. Muon = 1/9 the proton mass and is routinely treated in DFT as an ultralight isotope of hydrogen, with implanted-muon anharmonic quantum effects now first-principles computable (Phys. Rev. B 'Anharmonic quantum effects of implanted muons', arXiv:2408.00185; muon-stopping-site quantum delocalization arXiv:1307.0651). The inverse isotope effect — LIGHTER sublattice gives HIGHER Tc via zero-point anharmonicity — is measured & ab-initio reproduced in PdH/PdD (arXiv:1305.7123) and SrPdH/D (arXiv:2602.23691). The lever: substitute a fraction of the H sublattice in an H3S-class hydride with a muonic-hydrogen (μ⁻ bound to a nucleus, i.e. a 'fat heavy electron' that screens like a 207× heavier electron) so the screening-particle ZPM amplitude lands in a window deeper than electron-screened but shallower than proton-mass-pinned. SPECULATIVE that this is a stationary engineerable state vs a transient muon-decay artifact.
falsifiable_prediction: An SSCHA (stochastic self-consistent harmonic approximation) + anharmonic-Eliashberg calculation of an H3S supercell with one H replaced by a μ-mass screening orbital will show λ (e-ph coupling) increasing monotonically as the mediator mass is swept from m_e upward toward m_μ⁻-screened, peaking at an effective mediator mass m* in the 10-40 m_e window, then collapsing as it approaches m_proton. If λ is flat or monotonically decreasing across the entire m_e→m_p sweep, the mass-axis lever is dead.
beats_phase_stiffness_ceiling: It does NOT route through spin fluctuations at all — it is a conventional Migdal-Eliashberg (phonon-class) glue whose ω_log ceiling is set by the mediator's zero-point frequency, not by Emery-Kivelson phase stiffness. The 134-164 K wall is a magnon/spin-fluctuation ceiling; a deeper-attraction anharmonic hydride sidesteps it the way H3S (203 K) already exceeded it. The mass lever attacks the OTHER ceiling — the <200 meV phonon ceiling — by lowering the mediator mass below the proton so ω_ZP rises above the H-phonon cutoff while keeping attraction (heavier-than-electron screening) intact.
novelty: All 5 surveyed boson-glue families fix the mediator IDENTITY (phonon/exciton/plasmon/magnon) and tune its frequency or coupling. This tunes the mediator's MASS as a continuous free parameter between electron and proton — a dimension none of them expose. It is not flat-band geometry (no quantum-metric term), not driven (stationary), not topological. Orthogonal to the phase-stiffness ceiling because it is a phonon-class, not fluctuation-class, glue.

name: Bipolaron Effective-Mass Dial (anharmonic ZPM minimum)
basis: GROUNDED kernel. The anharmonic theory of superconductivity (Setty et al., arXiv:2303.12977) shows anharmonic lattice glue has a non-monotonic Tc vs mass/anharmonicity. Real result: in PdH the inverse isotope effect proves Tc(mass) has a slope of the 'wrong' sign — i.e. there exists a mass at which dTc/dm changes sign, an EXTREMUM. SPECULATIVE extension: treat the screening-particle effective mass m* (set by isotope/muonic substitution or by a heavy-electron host band hybridizing with the H ZPM) as a continuous dial and seek the global Tc(m*) maximum — the 'sweet-spot mass' where bipolaron radius is large enough to avoid self-trapping (light) yet attraction is deep enough to bind (heavy). This is the bipolaron crossover (Alexandrov-Mott), now reframed on the muon-to-proton mass continuum.
falsifiable_prediction: Tc as a function of mediator effective mass m* in a fixed hydride lattice will be non-monotonic with a single interior maximum; in-silico SSCHA sweeps of isotope mass (μ-H, H, D, T as discrete m* points, plus fictitious-mass interpolation) will bracket a peak at m* < m_H. Concretely: predict Tc(μ-H-doped lattice) > Tc(H) > Tc(D) > Tc(T) with the μ point already on the rising side of the peak. Falsified if the ordering is monotone in the BCS-conventional direction (heavier = higher Tc) for ALL four.
beats_phase_stiffness_ceiling: Bipolaron superfluid density (and hence phase stiffness) scales as n/m*_bipolaron. The spin-fluctuation ceiling at 134-164 K is fundamentally a LOW phase-stiffness problem (Emery-Kivelson: T_c bounded by superfluid stiffness, not by the gap). Dialing the mediator mass DOWN raises the bipolaron mobility / lowers m*_bipolaron, directly raising stiffness ρ_s ∝ 1/m* — attacking the exact quantity that caps the magnon route, but via a light-mass real-space-pair mechanism instead of a fluctuation mechanism.
novelty: The magnon family hits the stiffness wall because spin-fluctuation pairs are heavy/incoherent. This proposes mediator-mass as the knob on stiffness ITSELF, not on the gap — inverting which quantity you optimize. Orthogonal to flat-band (which boosts stiffness via quantum metric of a FLAT band; this boosts it via a LIGHT pair mass in a dispersive band). Not on any surveyed list.

name: Positronium-Channel Two-Boson Resonance
basis: PURE-SF construction on a partly-real chassis. Real: photon-mediated e⁻e⁺ interaction is ATTRACTIVE (unlike e⁻e⁻), and two-boson exchange can be resonantly amplified when the mediator frequency approaches an interband transition (Theory of resonantly enhanced photo-induced superconductivity, PMC10940728; composite-boson mediators cond-mat/9509019). SPECULATIVE-to-SF: in a positron-rich solid (e.g. positron-trapping vacancy lattice) a virtual positronium (Ps) bound state — mass 2m_e, the lightest possible composite boson — mediates an effective e⁻-e⁻ attraction via two-photon / Ps-exchange, with the Ps reduced mass (m_e/2) setting an exchange frequency far ABOVE any phonon. The lifetime problem (Ps annihilates in ns) is the honest killer — this only works if VIRTUAL (off-shell) Ps exchange dominates, never real Ps formation.
falsifiable_prediction: A diagrammatic / Eliashberg-kernel calculation of two-photon-exchange-via-virtual-Ps in a model positron-doped lattice predicts a pairing kernel peaked at ω ≈ Ps binding energy (6.8 eV)/ℏ scale, i.e. an attraction-mediating frequency >> 200 meV, giving an Allen-Dynes Tc ceiling an order of magnitude above the phonon ceiling. Falsified if the virtual-Ps vertex correction is suppressed by the annihilation width Γ such that the real part of the kernel never becomes net-attractive for physical positron densities.
beats_phase_stiffness_ceiling: Completely bypasses BOTH ceilings: not a phonon (so not <200 meV), not a spin fluctuation (so not the 134-164 K stiffness wall). The mediator mass (2m_e) is the lightest possible, pushing ω_mediator into the eV range, so the Allen-Dynes prefactor ω_log is ~30× the magnon scale. Phase stiffness is not the binding constraint here because the glue is an electronic/leptonic resonance, not a slow magnetic mode — stiffness is set by the electron band, which is already stiff.
novelty: Orthogonal to all 5 boson families: the mediator is a leptonic composite boson (positronium), not phonon/exciton/plasmon/magnon, and its mass is 2m_e — below the muonic regime entirely, defining the LIGHT end of the mass axis the prompt named. The exciton family is DEAD because its order traps; Ps-exchange forms no static order (it is purely virtual), so the 'order traps' law does not apply.

name: Mediator-Mass Gradient Lattice (mass-engineered ZPM funnel)
basis: SPECULATIVE, grounded components. Real: muonium acts as an H analogue with quantum effects strongly site-dependent (cond-mat/9908220); muon stopping sites have spatially-varying anharmonic quantum delocalization (arXiv:1307.0651). The lever: build a hydride with a SPATIAL GRADIENT of screening-particle mass — e.g. a muonic-H-rich layer adjacent to a normal-H layer — so the ZPM amplitude varies across the cell, creating an attraction gradient that funnels pairs into a high-ω_log channel while a stiffer adjacent region supplies phase stiffness (a mass-axis analog of the surveyed multilayer D_s boost, but the boosted quantity is the MEDIATOR MASS, not the band geometry).
falsifiable_prediction: A bilayer hydride supercell with a μ-mass-screened sublayer + an H-mass sublayer will, in coupled-layer anharmonic Eliashberg, show a Tc exceeding BOTH homogeneous endpoints (μ-only and H-only) — a proximity enhancement where the light layer donates ω_log and the heavy layer donates stiffness. Falsified if Tc(bilayer) ≤ max(Tc_μ, Tc_H), i.e. no synergy, just averaging.
beats_phase_stiffness_ceiling: Spatial division of labor: the light-mediator region maximizes the gap/ω_log (attacking the phonon ceiling) while the heavy/dispersive-electron region supplies superfluid stiffness ρ_s, decoupling the two quantities that the magnon route is forced to co-optimize and fails. The 134-164 K wall arises when ONE phase must provide both glue and stiffness; spatially separating mediator masses lets stiffness come from electrons (eV-scale, far above 164 K) while glue comes from the light anharmonic mode.
novelty: Distinct from the surveyed multilayer D_s boost (that modulates band/quantum-geometry across layers); here the modulated parameter is the mediator's MASS along the muon-proton continuum. Distinct from demand-relaxation and 3D levers. It is the mass-axis realization of 'separate the glue from the stiffness,' which no surveyed family does because they all keep a single homogeneous mediator.

name: ZPE Flat-Bander (quantum-nuclear band-flattening via proton delocalization)
axis: isotope/nuclear-mass engineering as a geometry knob — proton zero-point delocalization reshapes the ELECTRONIC band geometry, not just the phonon spectrum
basis: GROUNDED kernel + SPECULATIVE extension. Real: SSCHA / quantum-nuclear anharmonicity demonstrably reshapes the Fermi surface in hydrides — in PdH/PtH/H3S zero-point proton motion increases intramolecular distance ~6% and opens NEW electron pockets / shifts band positions (Errea et al., 'Anharmonic theory of superconductivity', arXiv:2303.12977; npj Comput. Mater. s41524-026-01973-7, 2026). Real: in the small-polaron limit, lighter isotopes give a GIANT isotope effect on the polaron band TOPOLOGY and van Hove singularity, not mere bandwidth scaling (Alexandrov, cond-mat/0407217). SPECULATIVE synthesis: tune nuclear mass (H vs D vs muonic-H, or partial ¹H/²H mixing) so that the proton ZPE wavefunction self-consistently delocalizes onto a frustrated sublattice geometry whose EFFECTIVE electronic hopping integrals destructively interfere — manufacturing a quasi-flat electronic band as an emergent quantum-nuclear average rather than from lattice topology. The flatness is a nuclear-mass-controlled order parameter: heavier isotope = sharper proton = dispersive band, lighter = delocalized = flat.
falsifiable_prediction: In a fixed lattice (e.g. a hydride or hydrogen-sublattice frustrated framework), SSCHA-averaged electronic structure will show the quantum-nuclear-renormalized electronic bandwidth W_eff at the target band MONOTONICALLY narrows as nuclear mass decreases (¹H < ²H < ³H ordering of W_eff), with the integrated quantum-geometric tensor tr g of that band increasing by >=20% from D to H. If tr g and W_eff are isotope-INSENSITIVE (within SSCHA noise), the seed is dead.
beats_phase_stiffness_ceiling: The ~134-164K phase-stiffness ceiling is an Emery-Kivelson SPIN-fluctuation-glue wall — it assumes glue=spin fluctuation whose stiffness saturates. This seed does NOT use spin-fluctuation glue at all; it engineers band geometry so the superfluid weight D_s (geometric, ~ tr g x interaction) is set by nuclear-mass-tuned quantum geometry rather than by a magnetic energy scale. The phase stiffness ceiling is bypassed because the stiffness source is the isotope-controlled quantum-geometric term, which is bounded by an electronic (eV) scale, not a magnetic (10-40 meV) scale — in principle decoupling T_c-stiffness from the spin-fluctuation saturation entirely.
novelty: Surveyed flat-band work derives flatness from LATTICE TOPOLOGY (CoSn kagome, tr g=2.86) — a fixed, classical-nuclei band structure. This makes flatness a TUNABLE quantum-nuclear average: the SAME lattice gives different tr g for different isotopes. Orthogonal to the phonon-glue family (which uses nuclei as a boson bath, ceiling 200 meV) — here nuclei are not glue at all, they are a geometry actuator. Orthogonal to driven/Floquet (static, equilibrium, not power-bound).

name: Isotopic Quantum-Critical Spacer (¹⁸O-tuned paraelectric stiffness donor)
axis: nuclear-mass engineering as a geometry knob — isotope substitution moves a system across a quantum-paraelectric critical point, and the diverging polarizability acts as a geometric phase-stiffness reservoir for an adjacent superconducting layer
basis: GROUNDED. Real: in SrTiO3, ¹⁶O->¹⁸O substitution alone (a pure nuclear-mass change, NO chemistry) tips the quantum paraelectric across its ferroelectric quantum critical point (Itoh et al.); the SC dome sits on this FE-QCP and T_c is enhanced near it (Rischau/Stucky et al., 'A ferroelectric quantum phase transition inside the superconducting dome', Nat. Phys. s41567-017... / Nat. Commun. s41467-019-08693-1). The proximity to the FE-QCP is itself isotope-controlled. SPECULATIVE extension: use the ¹⁸O-tuned quantum-paraelectric as a PROXIMITY SPACER beneath/between flat-band layers — its diverging dielectric/polarizability screens the on-layer Coulomb (raising effective attraction) and its near-critical soft mode supplies a geometric, non-magnetic phase-stiffness channel to the SC layer through the spacer.
falsifiable_prediction: For a flat-band-layer / SrTi(¹⁶O vs ¹⁸O)3-spacer / flat-band-layer trilayer, the in-silico computed effective intralayer pairing attraction U_eff (via the spacer's RPA dielectric response) will be >=15% larger for the ¹⁸O (near-critical) spacer than the ¹⁶O spacer, AND the cross-spacer phase-coupling (Josephson-like) energy will track the spacer's static dielectric constant. If U_eff and phase-coupling are isotope-flat, dead.
beats_phase_stiffness_ceiling: The spin-fluctuation ceiling comes from magnon stiffness saturating ~134-164K. Here the stiffness/glue reservoir is a FERROELECTRIC soft mode at a quantum-critical point — a charge/lattice channel, not spin. Near the FE-QCP the relevant fluctuation susceptibility DIVERGES (that's the point of a QCP), so the available phase-stiffness reservoir is not bounded by a fixed magnetic exchange J; it is bounded only by how close isotope tuning places you to criticality. This is a different ceiling-setting quantity than Emery-Kivelson, so the magnon wall does not apply.
novelty: Surveyed connector/spacer work (H_009, H_011 glue-through-spacer) treats the spacer as a passive transparent medium or a chemical screen; surveyed demand-relaxation/multilayer work boosts D_s by stacking. NONE use ISOTOPE substitution in the spacer to position it at a quantum-critical point. Orthogonal to exciton (EI-DEAD) because the FE soft mode is a polar phonon-derived collective mode, not an exciton condensate; orthogonal to the magnon family by construction (non-magnetic QCP).

name: Mass-Mismatch Anharmonic Cloak (geometry-protecting isotopic anharmonicity against competing order)
axis: nuclear-mass engineering as a geometry knob — strong anharmonic zero-point motion DISORDERS a competing electronic order (CDW/lattice distortion) while LEAVING the flat-band geometry intact, using isotope mass as the tuning of the quantum melting
basis: GROUNDED kernel. Real: quantum nuclear ZPE is precisely what PREVENTS mode condensation / freezing — in SrTiO3 oxygen ZPE melts the ferroelectric distortion (quantum paraelectricity); SSCHA shows anharmonic ZPE stabilizes/destabilizes lattice distortions and CDWs (e.g. anharmonic suppression of charge-order / structural transitions, Errea-school SSCHA on NbS2/H3S; 'Anharmonic theory...' arXiv:2303.12977). Proton tunneling correlated with electrons directly observed in Pd (Sci. Adv. ady8495, 2025). SPECULATIVE synthesis: in a flat-band candidate that is sabotaged by a competing periodic lattice distortion (the campaign's deep law: 'the ORDER traps'), pick a LIGHT isotope so that quantum ZPE quantum-melts the trapping distortion (raising its quantum-critical melting above operating T) while the flat band — being a quantum-geometric, not distortion-locked, feature — survives. Isotope mass becomes the dial that separates the wanted geometry from the unwanted order.
falsifiable_prediction: In an SSCHA scan of a flat-band material with a competing static distortion, the distortion's quantum-anharmonic free-energy minimum will shift from broken-symmetry (¹H/heavy) toward UNDISTORTED (light isotope / muonic) at fixed T, while the flat band's tr g changes by <10% across the same isotope range. Concretely: there exists an isotope mass m* below which the competing-order amplitude collapses to <50% of harmonic value yet the flat band persists. If lightening the isotope kills BOTH the distortion AND the flat band together (they co-melt), the cloak fails and the seed is dead.
beats_phase_stiffness_ceiling: This seed does not raise stiffness directly — it REMOVES the competing-order trap that, in the campaign's law, eats the fluctuation glue. The 134-164K ceiling is partly an effective ceiling because the order that would otherwise condense competes with and traps the pairing channel; by quantum-melting that order with isotopic ZPE, the full geometric D_s (electronic-scale) is freed instead of being clamped at the spin-fluctuation scale. It beats the wall by deleting the clamp rather than out-muscling it.
novelty: Surveyed competing-order work (H_014 threshold, H_016 escape) treats competing order as a static energetic threshold to out-compete electronically or via doping. This is the FIRST to use quantum-nuclear ZERO-POINT melting (isotope-tuned) as the eraser, exploiting that ZPE disorders lattice-locked orders but not quantum-geometric bands. Orthogonal to disorder-flat-band (H_017) which uses static structural disorder — here the 'disorder' is dynamic quantum delocalization, reversible and geometry-selective. Directly operationalizes the campaign's 'order traps' law with a nuclear-mass knob no surveyed family used.

name: Vibronic Ratchet Antenna (the FMO-pair host)
basis: Real concept (recast). Photosynthetic light-harvesting (Fenna-Matthews-Olson complex) shows ENVIRONMENT-ASSISTED quantum transport: protein vibrational modes near-resonant with electronic gaps (Engel et al. 2007 Nature 'long-lived quantum coherence'; Plenio & Huelga 2008 'Dephasing-assisted transport'). The vibronic mode does not merely decohere — it acts as a non-Markovian energy reservoir that RE-PHASES the electronic dwell. SPECULATIVE recast: take that exact protein-pigment vibronic resonance condition (Huang-Rhys factor S~0.5, mode ~180 cm^-1 ~22 meV) as a PAIRING vertex between two carriers co-tunneling on a chromophore dimer, not as a single-exciton transport aid.
falsifiable_prediction: In a model dimer with a single quantized vibronic mode tuned to the electronic detuning (the FMO resonance condition), the two-particle (co-tunneling) channel acquires an effective attractive vertex whose binding energy PEAKS at the resonance and exceeds the single-particle dephasing rate by >2x; detuning the mode by >30% collapses the bound-pair spectral weight to zero. If the two-particle T-matrix shows no resonance-locked attractive pole, the seed is dead.
beats_phase_stiffness_ceiling: The ~134-164K wall is a PHASE-STIFFNESS (superfluid density) ceiling, not a binding ceiling. This seed does NOT raise stiffness in bulk — instead it argues the relevant scale is set by the vibronic re-phasing rate, which in proteins survives to 300K precisely because the protein is the bath that protects it (Huelga-Plenio mechanism is room-temperature-native). The escape is: stiffness is irrelevant if coherence length is set by a 300K-robust non-Markovian timescale rather than by an equilibrium order parameter. Honestly, this only beats the wall if a finite stiffness can be shown in a STACK of such dimers (see DNA-stack seed); a single wire has zero 3D stiffness.
novelty: The exciton family was closed because 'order traps' (the EI order parameter competes with SC). Here the vibronic mode is a FLUCTUATING (non-Markovian, non-ordered) reservoir, not a condensate — it never forms a competing order, so it sits on the campaign's 'fluctuation glues' side rather than the dead exciton-order side. Orthogonal to phonon(<200meV ceiling) because the relevant scale is the re-phasing rate, not the mode energy.

name: Helical Berry-Phase Spinor Wire (the chiral-DNA pairing host)
basis: Real concept (recast). Chiral-Induced Spin Selectivity (CISS): electron transport through chiral molecules (dsDNA, helical peptides) is strongly spin-polarized at ROOM TEMPERATURE (Naaman & Waldeck 2012 J. Phys. Chem. Lett.; Gohler et al. 2011 Science, dsDNA spin filter). The helix geometry + spin-orbit produces an effective momentum-dependent spin texture and a geometric (Berry) phase. SPECULATIVE recast: treat the CISS spin-momentum locking on a double-helix as a synthetic 1D spin-orbit-coupled wire whose pair susceptibility is enhanced in the odd-parity (triplet) channel by the helical geometry, analogous to how Rashba wires host equal-spin triplet correlations.
falsifiable_prediction: A tight-binding double-helix Hamiltonian with the measured CISS spin-orbit strength will, when proximitized, show a triplet pair susceptibility that diverges faster than the singlet channel as T->0, and the ratio scales monotonically with helical pitch chirality (flipping handedness suppresses one spin-triplet component). If the triplet/singlet susceptibility ratio is <1 across all physical pitches, dead.
beats_phase_stiffness_ceiling: Phase stiffness wall is set by spin-fluctuation glue (Emery-Kivelson, spin channel). An equal-spin TRIPLET condensate does not couple to the singlet spin-fluctuation pair-breaking that sets the 134-164K ceiling — it is in a different symmetry sector entirely. The escape: the ceiling is a property of the d-wave/singlet spin-fluctuation host; a geometric-CISS triplet wire is glued by spin-orbit/Berry-phase, which has no Emery-Kivelson stiffness penalty because there is no competing antiferromagnetic order to suppress n_s. Honest caveat: 1D triplet wires have notoriously fragile stiffness from quantum fluctuations; needs a bundle.
novelty: Magnon/spin-fluctuation family was closed at the singlet phase-stiffness wall. This is orthogonal because the glue is GEOMETRIC (Berry phase from helicity), not a boson exchange at all — it sidesteps the entire '5 boson glue families' axis. Also orthogonal to topology-as-independent-lever (closed-negative) because here chirality is the PAIRING vertex itself, not a post-hoc topological protection.

name: Self-Healing Stiffness Bundle (wet 3D phase-lock of soft wires)
basis: Real concept (recast) + SPECULATIVE. Liquid-liquid phase separation / biomolecular condensates (Hyman, Brangwynne 2014 Annu. Rev. Cell Dev. Biol.) self-assemble dense, dynamically-exchanging 3D meshes of biopolymers at 300K. Amyloid/peptide nanowire bundles (e.g. conductive pili of Geobacter, Malvankar/Lovley 2011 Nat. Nanotechnol. — measured metallic-like conductivity in protein nanowires) form aligned 1D conductive fibers. SPECULATIVE recast: use the condensate as a self-repairing 3D scaffold that Josephson-couples a dense ensemble of individually-weak coherent wires, so the 3D phase stiffness is an EMERGENT inter-wire coupling rather than an intra-wire property.
falsifiable_prediction: For an array of weak 1D pairing wires with inter-wire Josephson coupling J and per-wire pair-breaking rate, the bulk T_c set by 3D phase-locking scales as T_c ~ sqrt(J * E_intra) (geometric mean, XY-stack result) and a measurable wire density threshold exists below which 3D coherence never onsets. Prediction: a self-repairing scaffold raises effective T_c by re-forming broken inter-wire links faster than thermal disorder breaks them, so T_c is HIGHER under a finite link-turnover rate than in a static (defected) bundle. If T_c is monotonically degraded by ANY turnover, the self-repair thesis is dead.
beats_phase_stiffness_ceiling: This directly attacks the wall's mechanism: the 134-164K ceiling is an EQUILIBRIUM phase-stiffness limit assuming a static lattice where defects permanently pin/suppress n_s. A self-repairing (driven, dissipative, non-equilibrium) scaffold continuously anneals the phase-disordering defects, so the steady-state phase stiffness can exceed the equilibrium-static value for the same microscopic parameters. It does not raise the per-wire gap; it raises the EFFECTIVE n_s by removing the defect tax that Emery-Kivelson stiffness pays. This is the one seed that genuinely targets the stiffness number rather than relocating to another sector.
novelty: Orthogonal to multilayer D_s boost (static crystalline stacking) and 3D-lever (static geometry): here the 3D coupling is wet, dynamic, and self-annealing — a NON-EQUILIBRIUM stiffness source. Distinct from driven/Floquet (closed: transient/power-bound) because the drive is chemical (ATP/turnover-like, ambient, sustained), not a coherent EM field with a power-budget wall. The 'self-repair beats static-defect ceiling' claim is the orthogonal physics.

name: Proton-Polaron Conveyor (Grotthuss-glued bipolaron wire)
basis: Real concept (recast). Proton wires in proteins/water chains move charge by Grotthuss hopping (concerted H-bond reorganization) at 300K (Agmon 1995 Chem. Phys. Lett.; bacteriorhodopsin/green-fluorescent-protein proton wires). The proton's strong coupling to the polarizable H-bond network is a genuine high-energy bosonic bath (O-H stretch ~430 meV, librations ~100 meV). SPECULATIVE recast: an electronic carrier co-moving with a Grotthuss proton-polaron forms a composite (electron + proton-defect) bipolaron whose pairing glue is the proton-network reorganization energy — a clean, non-magnetic, high-energy boson native to wet soft matter.
falsifiable_prediction: A two-carrier model with electron-proton coupling at the measured Grotthuss reorganization energy will form a bound bipolaron with binding energy set by the proton-network polarization (predicted >50 meV, i.e. above the 200 meV-phonon-equivalent ceiling in temperature terms) and the binding will exhibit an H/D ISOTOPE SHIFT (deuteration lowers binding via slower Grotthuss transfer). No isotope-dependent binding pole => the proton glue is illusory and seed is dead.
beats_phase_stiffness_ceiling: The phonon family ceiling (<200 meV optical) is a lattice-mode limit; the O-H stretch / proton-network reorganization is a HIGHER-energy bosonic channel (~430 meV stretch) that is uniquely accessible in H-bonded soft matter and absent in hard crystals. Beating the wall: binding scale set by proton reorganization energy exceeds the spin-fluctuation 134-164K equivalent, AND because it is a charge/proton (non-magnetic) glue, it pays no Emery-Kivelson spin-stiffness penalty. Honest limit: bipolarons famously have huge effective mass => tiny stiffness; this seed must be paired with the Self-Healing Bundle to have any 3D n_s at all.
novelty: Orthogonal to all 5 boson families: the glue boson is the PROTON-NETWORK reorganization mode (Grotthuss), not phonon/exciton/plasmon/magnon — a distinct degree of freedom that only exists in H-bonded wet matter, never surveyed. Distinct from the campaign's solid-state Fröhlich/bipolaron entry (that was lattice-phonon polarons with no high-Tc precedent) because the boson here is a chemical proton-transfer mode at ~430 meV with a sharp isotope-effect falsifier.

name: ENZ Screening Inversion (the Permittivity Mirror)
axis: METAMATERIAL / EFFECTIVE-MEDIUM — bulk epsilon-near-zero cladding re-engineers the screened Coulomb kernel V(q,w)=v(q)/eps(q,w), no cavity mode
basis: REAL concept. Near an epsilon-near-zero (ENZ) frequency the effective host permittivity passes through zero and goes negative on the upper side; since the screened Coulomb interaction is V = v_bare/eps, a region where Re[eps]<0 inverts the SIGN of the static-screened electron-electron interaction (repulsion -> attraction) WITHOUT invoking any phonon/exciton/plasmon GLUE MODE — it is a property of the medium's dielectric function, not of an exchanged boson. ENZ media and their eps-crossing are experimentally established (Engheta, 'Pursuing Near-Zero Response,' Science 2013; Liberal & Engheta, Nat. Photonics 2017). The reuse of eps<0 to flip the Coulomb sign in a pairing context is SPECULATIVE — distinct from cited plasmonic-glue work (in 't Veld/Rosner arXiv:2508.06195) which ADDS a hybridized plasmon mode rather than driving the host eps through zero as a passive cladding.
falsifiable_prediction: If a 2D electron layer is clad in an effective medium engineered so that Re[eps(q->0, w~0)] is small-and-negative over the relevant electron-momentum window, the cRPA/constrained static screened interaction W(q,0) computed for the embedded layer must show a sign reversal (W<0, attractive) for q below a cutoff q*; absent the cladding, W>0 everywhere. Falsified if the embedded W(q,0) stays strictly positive for all engineerable eps<0 windows, or if the q-window where W<0 collapses to q=0 measure-zero (no finite phase space).
beats_phase_stiffness_ceiling: The ~134-164K spin-fluctuation ceiling is a PHASE-STIFFNESS (D_s) wall set by a fluctuation glue whose ordered cousin traps carriers. An ENZ-inverted STATIC Coulomb attraction is not a fluctuation and carries NO competing order to trap — it is a real (non-retarded) attraction, so pairing scale is set by the bare interaction strength (potentially ~eV) rather than a soft-mode energy capped near magnon bandwidth. Stiffness is set by the carrier superfluid weight, not by the glue's ordering temperature, so the Emery-Kivelson fluctuation-stiffness tradeoff that produces the ceiling does not apply.
novelty: Orthogonal to all 5 boson-glue families because it provides NO exchanged boson — it reshapes eps(q,w) of the environment so the EXISTING Coulomb term becomes attractive. Distinct from the surveyed plasmon-glue research (which couples to a hybridized plasmon mode) and from cavity-QED seeds (no discrete cavity mode; a bulk effective-medium cladding). Distinct from flat-band/quantum-geometry levers which act on the band, not the interaction kernel.

name: Hyperbolic Pitch-Black Coulomb (Anisotropic Negative-Group-Velocity Glue Channel)
axis: METAMATERIAL / EFFECTIVE-MEDIUM — hyperbolic-dispersion cladding (eps_parallel and eps_perp opposite signs) opens a high-k attractive channel in the screened interaction
basis: REAL concept. Hyperbolic metamaterials have a permittivity tensor with components of opposite sign (eps_xx>0, eps_zz<0 or vice versa), giving an open (hyperbolic) iso-frequency surface that supports propagating modes out to very large in-plane momenta k (the high-k 'Dyakonov/volume-plasmon-polariton' continuum). Real and reviewed (Poddubny, Iorsh, Belov, Kivshar, 'Hyperbolic metamaterials,' Nat. Photonics 2013; Caldwell et al. on natural hyperbolic hBN, Nat. Commun. 2014). The high-k photonic density of states diverges. SPECULATIVE step: embedding the electron layer so that the hyperbolic high-k continuum overlaps the electron Fermi-momentum window provides an effective-medium screening that is ATTRACTIVE precisely at the large-q (short-distance, pairing-relevant) sector where ordinary screening is weak — a momentum-selective attraction, no discrete cavity mode.
falsifiable_prediction: For an electron layer at the surface of a hyperbolic cladding (e.g. hBN-on-metal effective stack), the momentum-resolved screened interaction W(q,w~0) must develop an attractive lobe at large q (q ~ k_F) that is ABSENT for an isotropic dielectric of the same average eps. Falsified if W(q) anisotropy from the hyperbolic tensor produces no sign change at any q, or if the attractive lobe sits at q far above 2k_F (no scattering phase space connecting Fermi-surface points).
beats_phase_stiffness_ceiling: The ceiling is a long-wavelength phase-stiffness limit tied to soft fluctuation glue. A hyperbolic channel delivers attraction at LARGE q (short distance), producing tightly-bound real-space pairs whose binding energy is set by the high-k photonic-DOS divergence (large), while the pair stiffness is decoupled from any magnetic ordering temperature. Because no spin order is invoked, there is no ordered-phase carrier trap; the stiffness ceiling that Emery-Kivelson assigns to fluctuation glue is replaced by the carrier kinetic/geometric superfluid weight, which can exceed 164K if pairs stay light.
novelty: Distinct axis: momentum-SELECTIVE (high-k) attraction from an anisotropic effective-medium tensor — none of the surveyed glue families (all roughly q~0 soft modes) nor the flat-band geometry lever touch the large-q sector of the interaction kernel. Orthogonal to the ENZ seed (that one is q->0, eps-zero-crossing; this one is large-q, hyperbolic-tensor). Not a cavity mode; a bulk birefringent effective medium.

name: Frequency-Dependent Anti-Screening Notch (Dynamical eps<1 Overshoot)
axis: METAMATERIAL / EFFECTIVE-MEDIUM — engineered dynamical eps(w) with a sub-unity / negative window placed at the electron pairing frequency, reshaping retardation without supplying a mode
basis: REAL building blocks. Any Lorentz-resonant effective medium has a frequency window just above resonance where Re[eps(w)]<1 and can dip below 0 (anomalous dispersion / Reststrahlen band); phonon-polariton crystals (SiC, hBN) and designed metamaterials exhibit engineerable Reststrahlen bands (Caldwell et al., Nanophotonics 2015). The Kramers-Kronig-constrained eps(w) overshoot is established physics. SPECULATIVE: positioning the effective-medium resonance so the eps<1 (anti-screening) notch coincides with the electron pairing energy scale enhances the retarded attraction (Morel-Anderson mu* reduction by environment, not by a host phonon) — an effective-medium analog of Coulomb-pseudopotential engineering, with the resonance external to the superconductor and tunable by structure rather than chemistry.
falsifiable_prediction: Embedding the electron system adjacent to a metamaterial with a Reststrahlen notch at frequency w_notch must lower the effective Coulomb pseudopotential mu*(embedded) below mu*(bare) by an amount that scales with the notch depth (1 - Re[eps_min]); a structured eps(w) with deeper/sharper notch yields monotonically smaller mu* in an Eliashberg-with-environment calculation. Falsified if mu* is insensitive to the external eps(w) notch (i.e. the environment cannot enter the pairing kernel because the layer is too well internally screened), or if causality (KK) forces a compensating positive-screening lobe that cancels the gain over the integrated kernel.
beats_phase_stiffness_ceiling: It does not add glue energy; it reduces the repulsive mu* that normally suppresses T_c, so an EXISTING modest attraction yields a higher T_c than the bare bound. Because the lever is on the Coulomb-repulsion side (mu*) rather than the glue-stiffness side, it sidesteps the fluctuation-stiffness tradeoff entirely: stiffness is unchanged, but the gap equation's effective coupling rises. It can stack on top of any non-magnetic glue, so it is not bounded by the spin-fluctuation ordering scale.
novelty: Targets the mu* (Coulomb pseudopotential / repulsion) side of the gap equation via an external structured dielectric, not the attractive-glue side — none of the surveyed families do environment-driven mu* suppression. Distinct from the ENZ static-sign-flip seed (this is a dynamical, retardation/KK-shaped notch tuned to pairing frequency, not a w~0 static inversion) and from the hyperbolic large-q seed (this is q~0, w-resonant). Not a cavity mode and not a boson glue.

name: Effective-Medium Vacuum-Permittivity Dressing (Bulk Casimir-Polder Pair Glue)
axis: METAMATERIAL / EFFECTIVE-MEDIUM — structured medium reshapes the broadband electromagnetic vacuum (modified photon Green's function) seen by two electrons, producing a vacuum-fluctuation-mediated attraction without any single resonant mode
basis: PARTIALLY-REAL / SPECULATIVE. The electromagnetic vacuum's dyadic Green's function is modified by a structured medium (the basis of Casimir/Casimir-Polder force engineering and the macroscopic-QED Lifshitz formalism — Scheel & Buhmann, 'Macroscopic QED,' Acta Phys. Slov. 2008; Casimir-force sign control via metamaterials proposed by Leonhardt & Philbin, New J. Phys. 2007). The pairwise interaction between charges integrated over the FULL modified-vacuum spectrum (not a single mode) is a broadband, retardation-corrected potential. SPECULATIVE: engineer the effective medium so the integrated vacuum-dressed two-electron potential acquires a net attractive tail — a 'bulk Casimir-Polder glue.' This is genuinely mode-free (broadband vacuum), unlike cavity-QED.
falsifiable_prediction: The two-electron interaction computed from the medium-modified photon Green's function (Lifshitz/macroscopic-QED kernel) for an engineered structured cladding must show a net-attractive integrated tail at electron separations of order the inter-carrier spacing, with magnitude tunable by the cladding's broadband reflectivity spectrum. Falsified if, after integrating the full causal (KK-consistent) vacuum spectrum, the medium-dressed potential remains repulsive at all relevant separations for every passive (positive-imaginary-eps) cladding — i.e. passivity forbids a net attractive bulk glue.
beats_phase_stiffness_ceiling: A vacuum-fluctuation-mediated attraction has NO material ordering temperature and NO competing density/spin order to trap carriers, so the 'fluctuation glues / order traps' law has no ordered cousin to invoke — the binding scale is set by the broadband vacuum spectral weight (potentially set by an eV-scale UV cutoff of the structured medium), wholly decoupled from the magnon-bandwidth-limited phase-stiffness wall.
novelty: Most orthogonal of the set: the glue is the structured electromagnetic VACUUM (broadband, mode-free), addressed via the macroscopic-QED/Lifshitz Green's function — categorically different from any exchanged-boson family, from the cavity-QED seeds (single mode), and from the other three metamaterial seeds (which reshape eps for material charge response; this reshapes the vacuum photon propagator itself). Flagged honestly as the most speculative.

name: Antadiabatic Paraelectric Saddle (the un-trapped soft mode)
axis: PIEZO/FLEXOELECTRIC/STRAIN-GRADIENT DYNAMIC LATTICE — quantum-paraelectric critical-point glue
basis: GROUNDED-ish. SrTiO3 is the canonical dilute quantum-paraelectric superconductor: pairing is enhanced as the ferroelectric soft transverse-optic mode is tuned toward its quantum critical point, with Tc peaking near the QCP (Edge/Levchenko/Chubukov/Triebl PRL 115,247002 (2015), arXiv:1508.07871; Rischau et al. Nat.Phys.13,643; Stucky et al. Sci.Rep.6,37582 on 18O/strain tuning). The campaign already logged the SrTiO3 polar phonon as a ~90 meV hard-phonon glue (research-clean-glue-candidates), but treated it as an ordinary phonon. The orthogonal claim: near the QCP the relevant scale is NOT the bare phonon energy but the DIVERGING static dielectric constant epsilon(0)~10^4 and vanishing soft-mode frequency, which screens Coulomb repulsion (mu* collapse) rather than supplying a high boson energy. The campaign's deep law 'fluctuation glues, order traps' is uniquely SATISFIED here: the paraelectric phase by definition never orders (zero-point fluctuations suppress the transition), so unlike the magnon/exciton/CDW cases there is NO competing static order to trap — the soft mode is the fluctuation that never freezes.
falsifiable_prediction: In a 2-band ED / Eliashberg toy with a soft TO mode of frequency omega_s tuned toward zero at fixed coupling, the dimensionless pairing eigenvalue lambda_eff and the screened mu* should follow lambda_eff ~ g^2/omega_s^2 * chi(T) with a Tc that PEAKS at a finite small omega_s and then COLLAPSES as omega_s->0 (the dome of Edge et al.). Prediction: the peak Tc over the whole soft-mode-tuning axis stays BELOW ~40 K at 1 atm because the dome maximum is bounded by quantum fluctuations cutting off chi before omega_s reaches zero. If a clean soft-mode ED can be tuned to exceed ~40 K without invoking carrier density beyond the dilute SrTiO3 regime, the seed is promoted; otherwise the QCP-glue is dome-ceilinged like every other boson.
beats_phase_stiffness_ceiling: It does NOT beat the phase-stiffness wall and is honest about it — SrTiO3 SC is dilute (low n), so its superfluid stiffness is small and it lands FAR below 134 K (measured Tc ~0.4 K). The only conceivable route over the wall is to decouple the dielectric-enhancement glue (which needs proximity to the QCP, not low carrier density) from the carrier density: a HEAVILY-doped paraelectric where epsilon is still large at the relevant q would raise n (hence stiffness) while keeping the mu*-collapse glue. Honest assessment: doping away from the QCP kills epsilon(0), so glue and stiffness trade off — likely the same ledger-deficit wall in new coordinates. Beats the ceiling ONLY if epsilon stays divergent at finite doping at large q (a flexoelectric, gradient-coupled channel — see next seed), which is the testable crack.
novelty: Orthogonal to all 5 surveyed boson families because the glue is a COLLAPSING DIELECTRIC (Coulomb-screening / mu* suppression), not a high-energy boson exchanged at its own frequency. Unlike magnon/exciton/CDW (where the boson IS the soft mode of a competing order that traps), the paraelectric soft mode is the soft mode of an order that QUANTUM FLUCTUATIONS FORBID from forming — the unique case where the campaign's 'order traps' clause is structurally void. Not the SrTiO3-as-hard-phonon entry already logged.

name: Flexoelectric Gradient Glue (pairing from strain-gradient, not strain)
axis: PIEZO/FLEXOELECTRIC/STRAIN-GRADIENT DYNAMIC LATTICE — flexoelectric coupling as an independent pairing channel
basis: SPECULATIVE (mechanism), grounded in real flexoelectricity. Flexoelectricity is the universal coupling of polarization to a STRAIN GRADIENT P_i = mu_ijkl d(eps_jk)/dx_l, present in EVERY material including centrosymmetric ones (unlike piezoelectricity), and DIVERGENTLY large near a paraelectric QCP because the flexocoupling coefficient scales with epsilon(0) (Zubko/Catalan/Tagantsev Annu.Rev.Mater.Res.43,387 (2013); Yudin/Tagantsev review). SPECULATIVE leap: treat the flexoelectric coupling of electrons to the gradient of the soft-mode displacement as a pairing vertex that is FINITE-q-peaked (it couples to dq*u(q), so the vertex ~ q*g, vanishing at q=0 but growing at large q). This evades the q->0 dielectric problem of the previous seed: the pairing weight is concentrated at finite q where it can give a sign-changing (d-wave-like) gap, and it does not require the bulk static dielectric to diverge — only the soft-mode susceptibility at finite q. No paper proposes flexoelectric Cooper pairing; this is the invented mechanism.
falsifiable_prediction: Build the pairing kernel V(q) = (q*mu)^2 * chi_soft(q,omega) in a 2D ED/BdG toy and solve the gap equation. Prediction: because the flexo vertex ~q vanishes at q=0, the leading instability is a FINITE-q, sign-changing (extended-s or d) gap, and the resulting lambda is at most O(1) for realistic mu (~nC/m), giving Tc < ~50 K. Falsifier: if the finite-q flexo kernel cannot produce lambda>0.3 for any physical mu and chi, the independent channel is dead; if it can AND the gap is nodeless on the Fermi surface, it is a genuinely new pairing route worth a card.
beats_phase_stiffness_ceiling: Plausible crack: the phase-stiffness ceiling (Emery-Kivelson) is a property of the LOW-carrier-density condensate (small D_s).
