A flexoelectric/finite-q channel does not intrinsically require low carrier density — it works in a metal with normal n if the soft mode survives. If the glue can operate at metallic carrier density (large n, hence large D_s) while the soft-mode susceptibility supplies the pairing, then stiffness and glue are decoupled and the 134 K wall (which is a small-D_s wall) need not bind. The likely closer: a metal screens the soft mode (Landau damping kills chi_soft), so the glue dies exactly where the stiffness would be high — testable as whether chi_soft(q) survives at metallic n in the toy.
novelty: Fully orthogonal: a STRAIN-GRADIENT (flexoelectric) pairing vertex is in none of the 5 boson families, the flat-band/topology levers, or the driven/Floquet set. Its defining feature — a vertex that VANISHES at q=0 and grows with q — is the inverse of every surveyed glue (all peaked at small q / the order wavevector). It is also the only channel that exists in centrosymmetric hosts without piezoelectricity.

name: Self-Straining Auxetic Pump (lattice that holds its own gradient)
axis: PIEZO/FLEXOELECTRIC/STRAIN-GRADIENT DYNAMIC LATTICE — a self-straining host (OBSTACLE-as-resource: built-in gradient)
basis: SPECULATIVE / pure-sf at the device level, grounded in two real ingredients. (1) Negative-Poisson-ratio (auxetic) and negative-thermal-expansion frameworks (e.g. ScF3, Cd(CN)2; Dove/Fang reviews) sustain large STATIC internal strain gradients at structural phase boundaries. (2) Flexoelectricity converts a static gradient into a built-in polarization field. SPECULATIVE synthesis: a host engineered to carry a permanent, spatially-periodic strain-gradient texture (a 'flexoelectric superlattice') would present electrons with a static, periodic flexo-potential — an emergent pairing-favorable modulation that does NOT need an external drive (unlike Floquet) and is not a competing electronic order (unlike CDW). The gradient is a STRUCTURAL/elastic degree of freedom, decoupled from the electronic order parameter, so 'the order' that would trap is mechanical, not electronic — it cannot pre-empt SC the way a CDW does. No literature proposes this; the auxetic+flexo+SC combination is invented.
falsifiable_prediction: Model a tight-binding lattice with a static periodic flexoelectric potential V_flexo(r) = lambda * grad(eps(r)) superimposed, and ask whether it generates a flat or near-flat band (quasi-1D channels at the gradient maxima) AND a pairing-favorable DOS peak. Prediction: a periodic strain-gradient texture WILL flatten bands (acts like a periodic potential), but the resulting flat-band SC is then STIFFNESS-LIMITED and falls under the already-frozen ~134 K phase-stiffness wall (research-geometric-stiffness-vs-phase-ceiling). Falsifier: if the self-strained band structure produces a high-DOS metallic band (NOT an isolated flat band) with carrier density high enough for D_s above the cuprate scale, it escapes; if it only reproduces a low-stiffness flat band, it is absorbed into the existing wall and the seed closes negative.
beats_phase_stiffness_ceiling: Attempted route: by making the pairing modulation STRUCTURAL/elastic rather than electronic, the carrier density is set independently of the glue, so in principle a high-n metal could carry the gradient texture and have large D_s. The honest pre-judgment (consistent with the frozen wall) is that periodic potentials tend to flatten bands and thus REDUCE stiffness, landing right back under the 134 K ceiling — the seed most likely confirms the wall rather than breaking it. It would beat the ceiling only if the gradient texture raises DOS without flattening (a dispersive high-DOS band), which the toy can directly check.
novelty: Orthogonal to the surveyed set: it is a STATIC, self-sustained strain-gradient (no external drive, so not Floquet/transient/power-bound) using an elastic/auxetic structural order as the resource, distinct from CDW (electronic), flat-band geometry (the band IS the lever here, gradient-induced), and all boson glues. It is the M4-OBSTACLE meta-principle applied to elasticity, an axis not in the brainstorm pool.

name: Internal-Negative-Pressure Cage (chemical tension stuffing)
basis: REAL concept: 'chemical precompression' is Ashcroft's original 1968/2004 rationale for metallic-hydrogen-like SC in hydrogen-rich compounds (Ashcroft PRL 2004, 'Hydrogen Dominant Metallic Alloys') — heavy atoms squeeze the H sublattice to megabar-equivalent density at far lower external P. The orthogonal twist (SPECULATIVE): instead of EXTERNAL compression, engineer the host lattice so the equilibrium cell is already under built-in INTERNAL pressure on the H/light sublattice via guest-atom radius mismatch (oversized cation forced into an undersized cage), i.e. negative-pressure framing — the cage is in tension while the active sublattice is in compression. Documented analog: 'chemical pressure' from isovalent substitution (e.g. La->Y/Ce in RH10) is a measured, real lever; using radius-mismatch frustration to put ONE sublattice under net compression at zero external P is the speculative extension.
falsifiable_prediction: For a guest-stuffed clathrate template (e.g. an oversized-cation variant of the M-B3C3 or M-H cage family), DFT vc-relax at 0 GPa yields a hydrogen/light-sublattice nearest-neighbor distance and projected H-DOS at E_F within 10% of the externally-precompressed (>=150 GPa) reference phase, AND retains 0 imaginary phonon modes at 1 atm. Falsified if the cage simply expands the H-sublattice back to ambient bond length (relieving the internal pressure) OR if soft modes appear.
beats_phase_stiffness_ceiling: It does NOT fight the spin-fluctuation 134-164K phase-stiffness ceiling at all — it routes around it. That ceiling is the Emery-Kivelson wall for FLUCTUATION-glued (magnon/spin) pairing where superfluid stiffness, not gap, is limiting. Hydride/covalent precompression is BCS/Eliashberg phonon pairing with a HUGE carrier density and high superfluid stiffness (n_s/m* large, omega_log ~1000-1200 K as already measured for CaH6/H3S in-ledger) — its stiffness ceiling is set by the electron-phonon condensate, not by a dilute-spin reservoir. CaH6 at 255K already proves stiffness is not the wall for this family; the only wall is the PRESSURE, which this seed attacks chemically.
novelty: Surveyed list closes the 5 boson-glue families and the flat-band/3D/multilayer geometry levers, all of which are stiffness- or glue-ceiling bound. The entire precompression family (phonon glue at megabar-equivalent density) is ABSENT from the surveyed seed pool, which only names phonons as the <=200meV-ceiling soft-mode glue — it never names the H-optical-phonon-at-megabar-density regime that has the one MEASURED >250K SC (LaH10). This seed is the negative-pressure-frame realization of that, distinct from the campaign's existing isostructural-swap decks (which keep external P) by demanding the compression be delivered by built-in lattice tension at 0 GPa.

name: Anharmonic Cage Freeze (quartic stabilization recovers the metastable megabar phase at 1 atm)
basis: REAL concept: strong quartic anharmonicity is what dynamically stabilizes high-Tc hydride cages — the SSCHA (stochastic self-consistent harmonic approximation, Errea/Mauri Nature 2020 for LaH10, PdH, H3S) shows harmonic DFPT badly mis-predicts the stable structure and that anharmonic phonon renormalization both stabilizes the cage and SETS Tc. SPECULATIVE extension: deliberately dope the cage with a heavy 'rattler' guest (filled-skutterudite / type-I clathrate 'rattling' guest physics is REAL — Nolas, Slack thermoelectric clathrates) whose large-amplitude anharmonic motion provides the quartic stiffening that lets a megabar-only phase remain a deep local minimum at 1 atm (metastable but dynamically frozen).
falsifiable_prediction: For a rattler-doped hydride/covalent cage, SSCHA (or frozen-phonon quartic fit) at 0 GPa shifts all harmonic-imaginary modes to real (positive) frequency AND the anharmonically-renormalized structure stays within the basin of the high-P phase (RMS displacement < cage radius). Falsified if SSCHA confirms the harmonic instability (modes stay imaginary after anharmonic renormalization) or the structure relaxes to a distinct ambient phase.
beats_phase_stiffness_ceiling: Again sidesteps the magnon/spin-fluctuation 134-164K ceiling entirely — this is phonon pairing, not fluctuation glue, so Emery-Kivelson dilute-stiffness does not apply. The relevant question is not stiffness but whether the high-omega_log, high-lambda condensate survives at 1 atm. Anharmonicity here is OBSTACLE-AS-RESOURCE: the soft mode that would kill the cage at 1 atm is converted by quartic terms into the very thing that freezes the cage in place, preserving the >250K-capable electron-phonon spectral function without external P.
novelty: Orthogonal to every surveyed family: the surveyed phonon entry is explicitly bounded as a <=200meV harmonic-soft-mode glue with a ceiling, and the bipolaron/soft-mode entry (H_008/B6) condenses the soft mode as a CARRIER. This seed instead uses ANHARMONIC renormalization as a structural-metastability tool to keep a megabar phase at 1 atm — a structure-stability lever (SSCHA), not a glue or carrier lever. SSCHA-at-ambient for a deliberately rattler-stabilized hydride is absent from the seed pool and from the campaign's harmonic-DFPT-only stability gates (the ledger's stability checks are harmonic vc-relax + DFPT imag-mode counts, which Errea shows are exactly the wrong tool).

name: Epitaxial Pressure-Equivalence (biaxial misfit strain == hydrostatic GPa via the bulk modulus)
basis: REAL concept: biaxial epitaxial misfit strain maps onto an effective hydrostatic-pressure shift through the elastic tensor (the deformation-potential / Gruneisen relation), and strain-tuned SC is measured (FeSe/STO, strained cuprate/nickelate films, arXiv:2307.15828 kagome platform — all in-ledger research). SPECULATIVE extension: choose a substrate whose lattice misfit imposes the SAME volumetric strain on a thin hydride/covalent-cage film that ~tens-of-GPa hydrostatic pressure would, recovering the high-P electron-phonon spectral function in a 1-atm device geometry — pressure-as-geometry delivered by the interface, not an anvil.
falsifiable_prediction: For a coherently-strained few-layer film of an ambient-metastable cage material (e.g. a B3C3 or BeH-cage slab) on a lattice-mismatched substrate, slab DFT shows the misfit-induced volumetric strain shifts omega_log and lambda toward the high-P bulk values, raising the predicted Tc by >20K vs the relaxed-ambient slab. Falsified if the accessible coherent misfit (before dislocation relaxation, typically <3-4% before critical thickness) produces <5K Tc change, OR if the film relaxes plastically below the needed strain.
beats_phase_stiffness_ceiling: This family's ceiling is the PRESSURE/strain magnitude needed, not spin-fluctuation phase stiffness — so the 134-164K Emery-Kivelson wall is irrelevant by construction (it is a covalent/phonon condensate with intrinsically high n_s). The honest limit that REPLACES the stiffness ceiling: coherent biaxial strain caps out at a few percent before dislocation, equivalent to only ~tens of GPa, far short of the ~150-250 GPa hydrides need — so this seed is most credible for MARGINAL-pressure ambient cages (B3C3-class, needing only a stabilizing nudge) rather than full megabar hydrides.
novelty: The campaign's existing strain/gating research treats epitaxial strain purely as a DOPING/band-shift lever (moving the CoSn flat band across E_F) and as an exciton-tuning lever — never as a hydrostatic-pressure-EQUIVALENT for recovering a megabar phonon spectral function. This pressure-equivalence framing (strain converted to effective GPa via the bulk modulus to reconstruct a high-P el-ph spectrum at 1 atm) is orthogonal to both the flat-band geometry axis and the boson-glue axis, and is exactly the missing 'pressure-as-geometry delivered chemically' axis named in the prompt.

name: Guest-Charge Doublet (host cage geometry + guest f-electron charge reservoir as a two-job split)
basis: SPECULATIVE (no single paper proposes this exact split). Building block 1 is REAL: in hydride/B-C clathrates the heavy guest both precompresses the cage AND donates charge into the active sublattice's bonding/antibonding bands (e.g. La 3+ e-doping the B-C sigma/pi bands — already a stated hypothesis in the LaB3C3 ledger note). Building block 2 is REAL: f-electron guests (lanthanide/actinide) carry a dense, near-E_F charge reservoir. The SPECULATIVE synthesis: pick the guest to optimize the CHARGE/precompression product separately from the host that fixes cage GEOMETRY — a precompression analog of the campaign's bilayer 'division of labor', but within one host-guest crystal rather than two stacked layers.
falsifiable_prediction: Across an isostructural guest series in a fixed cage (vary guest = Ca/Sr/La/Ce/Th at the SAME Wyckoff site), DFT shows N(E_F) on the active (H or B-C) sublattice and the cage volumetric pre-strain trade off MONOTONICALLY and SEPARABLY, so an intermediate guest maximizes lambda*omega_log (hence Tc) above every end-member. Falsified if the two effects are locked (every guest that raises N(E_F) also over-expands the cage, relieving precompression) — i.e. if it collapses into a single coupled axis with one optimum at an end-member.
beats_phase_stiffness_ceiling: Phonon/covalent condensate => the spin-fluctuation 134-164K stiffness ceiling does not bind. The mechanism for exceeding it is to push lambda and omega_log up at 1-atm-equivalent conditions by independently tuning the charge-reservoir guest, raising the BCS/Eliashberg Tc the way external pressure does for LaH10 (already MEASURED >250K) but holding the geometry fixed by the host. The replacement ceiling is the separability assumption itself — the falsifier directly tests whether charge and precompression can be decoupled, which is the load-bearing claim.
novelty: The surveyed bilayer 'division of labor' (B2/H_004) splits geometry vs glue across two STACKED LAYERS via proximity. This seed splits precompression-geometry vs charge-reservoir across HOST vs GUEST within one host-guest crystal in the precompression family — a different physical realization (chemical, single-crystal, no proximity coupling) on an axis (chemical precompression) entirely absent from the seed pool. The campaign's existing M-B3C3 / M-H10 decks vary the guest but treat it as a single coupled knob with no Tc claim; this seed reframes the guest as an independently-optimizable charge axis decoupled from cage geometry, which is a novel and directly falsifiable prediction.

name: Ligand-Hole Negative-U: the pair lives on oxygen, not the cation
basis: GROUNDED. Varma's valence-skipper negative-U (Varma, PRL 61, 2713 (1988), 'Missing valence states, diamagnetic insulators, and superconductors') is conventionally pictured as on-cation charge skipping (Bi3+/Bi5+). But Foyevtsova, Khomskii et al. (PRB 91, 121114(R) (2015)) and Plumb et al. showed the BaBiO3 'charge disproportionation' is actually a LIGAND (O-2p) breathing-bond/hole-pair: the negative-U pair sits on the symmetric O6 octahedral molecular orbital, NOT localized on Bi. Anderson's negative-U center (PRL 34, 953 (1975)) generalized to a delocalized ligand orbital. The seed: deliberately engineer a valence-skipper host (Bi/Tl/Pb/Sb) where the negative-U pairing orbital is the MOST delocalized ligand MO available (long O-2p / S-3p / Te-5p networks, corner-sharing octahedra), so the bound pair has a small effective mass despite an eV-class on-site binding.
falsifiable_prediction: In a tight-binding + ED negative-U model on a BaBiO3-class breathing lattice, the bipolaron effective mass m* (from the pair dispersion of the two-particle Green function) scales as m* ∝ exp(+|U_eff|/W_ligand): increasing the ligand bandwidth W (more delocalized O-2p MO, e.g. by reducing octahedral tilt/expanding the M-O-M angle toward 180°) at FIXED |U_eff| drops m* below ~3 m_e, giving a BEC stiffness ρ_s ∝ n_pair/m* with T_BKT > 164 K — i.e. the pair binding (eV) and the stiffness become INDEPENDENTLY tunable. Falsified if m* stays >10 m_e for all W with bound pairing intact, i.e. binding and delocalization cannot be simultaneously achieved (the skipper self-traps).
beats_phase_stiffness_ceiling: The ~134-164 K wall is a phase-stiffness wall set by spin-fluctuation glue on LOW-stiffness cuprate hosts. Here the pairing scale is on-site chemical (eV, boson-free) so it is nowhere near the bottleneck — the only question is stiffness ρ_s = ħ²n_pair/(4 m* a²·...). A DELOCALIZED ligand negative-U pair is light AND the carrier density n_pair can be high (heavy doping of the skipper sublattice, as in Ba1-xKxBiO3), so ρ_s can exceed the cuprate 7.4 meV stiffness scale — the ceiling is cuprate/spin-fluctuation-specific, not universal, and chemistry that makes the pair light + dense routes around it.
novelty: Orthogonal to all 5 boson-glue families (no boson exchanged — attraction is on-site). Distinct from the flat-band geometric-stiffness lever (which is ALSO stiffness-limited and lands below the wall): here stiffness comes from a LIGHT REAL-SPACE pair, not from quantum metric of a flat band. Distinct from the bipolaron effective-mass dial seed (B6 / anharmonic-ZPM), which dials a PHONON soft mode; this dials the chemical disproportionation orbital's delocalization, no phonon.

name: Frustrated Valence Skipper: the disproportionation that can't crystallize
basis: SPECULATIVE extension of grounded facts. The campaign's deep law is 'the FLUCTUATION glues, the ORDER traps' — in BaBiO3 the negative-U is REAL but it ORDERS into a charge-density-wave (Bi3+/Bi5+ rock-salt CDW insulator); SC only appears when K-doping MELTS that CDW (Ba1-xKxBiO3, Sleight 1988). So the on-site pairing glue exists but its own ORDERED phase traps it — exactly the campaign's competing-order trap, now in the charge channel. The seed: put the valence-skipper ions on a GEOMETRICALLY FRUSTRATED lattice (pyrochlore / triangular / kagome of MO6 octahedra) so the checkerboard charge-disproportionation CDW is geometrically forbidden — the negative-U pairs stay as a FLUCTUATING charge liquid (pair-density fluctuation) that can only condense into the SC channel, the charge analog of frustrated magnetism feeding a spin liquid. Connects to H_022 (frustration unlock) but in the negative-U charge sector, not spin.
falsifiable_prediction: On a frustrated (pyrochlore/triangular) negative-U lattice, ED/QMC of the extended-Hubbard model with U_eff<0 and nearest-neighbor V (the inter-site charge repulsion that drives the CDW) shows the charge-ordered insulating gap COLLAPSES (no static CDW) while the s-wave pair susceptibility stays divergent — i.e. SC survives at a doping/V where the unfrustrated (cubic) lattice is a CDW insulator. Quantitatively: the SC dome's max pair binding accessible without CDW competition rises by the frustration factor (the H_022 unlock) so the pairing-limited Tc ceiling moves above the spin-fluctuation 164 K. Falsified if frustration instead opens a charge-glass/Wigner-localized gap (pairs localize) or a different (e.g. valence-bond) order traps them.
beats_phase_stiffness_ceiling: The 134-164 K wall is the spin-fluctuation phase-stiffness ceiling. This seed does not even enter the spin channel — it removes the COMPETING ORDER (charge CDW) that caps the negative-U pairing channel, letting the eV-class on-site pairing scale survive un-trapped. Stiffness is then set by a frustrated dense charge-liquid of light pairs (high n_pair from a melted-but-still-bound disproportionation), which has no a-priori reason to sit at the cuprate stiffness value. The wall is escaped by attacking the order-trap in a DIFFERENT channel than the one that built the wall.
novelty: Orthogonal to the 5 boson families and to flat-band/multilayer levers. It generalizes the campaign's competing-order escape (H_016) and frustration-unlock (H_022) into the negative-U CHARGE sector — a place the surveyed list never went (those were spin/magnon-channel). No boson; the glue is on-site chemistry, the lever is lattice geometry suppressing the charge-CDW trap.

name: Charge-Kondo / Pair-Resonance Lattice: a negative-U impurity band of preformed pairs
basis: GROUNDED core, SPECULATIVE lattice. A single negative-U center is a 'charge Kondo' problem (Taraphder & Coleman, PRL 66, 2814 (1991)): the two degenerate charge states (skipper's 2+ valences) map onto an isospin that gets Kondo-screened, producing a many-body PAIR RESONANCE pinned at the Fermi level. Dilute Tl-doped PbTe is the experimental charge-Kondo system (Matsushita/Fisher; Dzero & Schmalian, PRL 94, 157003 (2005) proposed it superconducts via this route). The seed: build a DENSE lattice of valence-skipper centers so the single-impurity pair resonances broaden into a narrow PAIR-RESONANCE BAND at E_F — a flat band whose carriers are ALREADY preformed local pairs (charge-2e bosons), so condensation is pure BEC of robustly pre-bound pairs and Tc is set by the band's coherence scale, not by a pair-breaking gap.
falsifiable_prediction: A lattice Anderson/extended negative-U model solved by DMFT (or ED on a cluster) shows a charge-Kondo pair-resonance band of width Γ at E_F whose two-particle spectral weight is dominantly charge-2e (preformed pairs). Prediction: the BEC condensation temperature T_c ≈ Γ/k_B (the resonance coherence scale) rises with skipper density and with hybridization V_pd, and exceeds 164 K when Γ > ~14 meV — achievable for strong p-d hybridization (Tl-O, Bi-O). Falsified if the pair resonance instead pins to a Kondo-insulating gap (no Fermi-level spectral weight → no condensation) or if increasing density destroys the resonance via inter-site charge order before Γ reaches 14 meV.
beats_phase_stiffness_ceiling: The 134-164 K wall is min(pairing, phase-stiffness) on a spin-fluctuation host. Here BOTH are reset: (a) pairing is the eV-class on-site negative-U / charge-Kondo binding — far above the wall; (b) the carriers are preformed pairs in a resonance band, so the relevant stiffness scale is the band coherence Γ (a CHARGE coherence, electronically broad p-d hybridized), not a soft cuprate superfluid density. Because the pairs never break (binding ≫ Γ), there is no pseudogap/pair-breaking competition — the campaign's 'order traps' failure mode is sidestepped, the condensation is set by an electronic eV-derived hybridization scale, which has no reason to obey the spin-fluctuation 7.4 meV stiffness number.
novelty: Orthogonal to all surveyed families: it is neither boson-glue, nor flat-band quantum-geometry (the flatness here is a many-body PAIR RESONANCE, not a single-particle band geometry → no quantum-metric ceiling), nor driven/topological. It is the charge-Kondo route (Taraphder-Coleman / Dzero-Schmalian), absent from the survey. Distinct from the bipolaron effective-mass / anharmonic-ZPM seed: no phonon, the pair is a Kondo charge resonance, an electronic many-body object.

name: NODAL-SPIN-SPLITTER GLUE (d-wave altermagnet pair vertex)
basis: GROUNDED concept, SPECULATIVE application. Altermagnetism (Smejkal, Sinova, Jungwirth, PRX 12, 040501 and 031042, 2022) = collinear compensated magnet with momentum-dependent, anisotropic (d/g/i-wave) spin splitting protected by spin-group symmetry, zero net moment. Real hosts: RuO2, MnTe, CrSb, KRu4O8. The pairing idea is that the spin-split bands provide a static, k-anisotropic spin-dependent vertex (NOT a soft Goldstone magnon). Altermagnet-mediated/altermagnet-host superconductivity is an active 2024-25 theory topic (e.g. arXiv:2309.01651 'Superconductivity in altermagnets', arXiv:2405.xxxx finite-momentum/unconventional pairing in altermagnets) — VERIFY exact IDs before citing in a card; mark SPECULATIVE if not confirmed.
falsifiable_prediction: In a 2-band altermagnet Hubbard/RPA model at half-filling, the spin-fluctuation pairing kernel evaluated on the d-wave-split Fermi surface yields an attractive eigenvalue lambda_pair whose magnitude does NOT scale with the magnon Goldstone stiffness rho_s of the parent order; specifically, tuning the Neel-vector stiffness toward zero (soft AFM limit) leaves lambda_pair finite (>0.3) because the splitting is set by the anisotropic hopping/crystal-field term, not by <S>. If lambda_pair collapses with rho_s the seed is falsified (it would just be a disguised magnon).
beats_phase_stiffness_ceiling: The ~134-164K ceiling comes from the glue being the soft Goldstone mode of a magnetic ORDER, so pairing strength J and phase stiffness rho_s share one root and collapse together (Emery-Kivelson). An altermagnet's spin splitting is enforced by SPIN-GROUP CRYSTAL SYMMETRY (anisotropic hopping between opposite-spin sublattices), not by a uniform exchange field with a soft Goldstone mode. The pairing vertex can therefore be large while the superconducting condensate's OWN charge phase stiffness (set by the carrier kinetic energy / band filling, decoupled from the magnetic Neel stiffness) is independently large — the two roots are SEPARATED, so the cuprate trade-off that pins rho_s low need not bind.
novelty: Every surveyed glue (phonon/exciton/plasmon/magnon) is a soft BOSON whose energy IS the order's instability; magnon explicitly closed because the high-energy spin wave IS the AFM soft mode (same trap as TiSe2). The altermagnet vertex is a STATIC band-structure spin texture, not a soft boson — it is not in the 5-boson list and is orthogonal to flat-band geometry (it is a Fermi-surface anisotropy, not a quantum-metric lever).

name: ZERO-MOMENT STIFFNESS LADDER (decoupled magnetic-vs-charge phase stiffness)
basis: GROUNDED premise. The campaign's universal wall is a PHASE-stiffness wall: T_BKT = (pi/2) D_s, and in cuprates/magnon-glue hosts the relevant D_s is suppressed because low carrier density and proximity to the magnetic instability share one tuning knob (Uemura/Homes scaling, Emery-Kivelson Nature 374,434). Altermagnets are typically GOOD METALS with zero net moment (RuO2 is a rutile metal with high carrier density) — SPECULATIVE claim is that an altermagnet can host high carrier density (large charge D_s) AND magnetic compensation (no moment to suppress carriers) simultaneously, unlike underdoped cuprates where doping away from the Mott insulator is what starves D_s.
falsifiable_prediction: Compute the Kelvin-equivalent charge superfluid stiffness D_s^eff for a metallic altermagnet candidate (RuO2-class carrier density n ~ 10^22 cm^-3) assuming a pairing gap from the seed-1 vertex: predict D_s^eff > 7.4 meV (the cuprate ~134K ceiling) because n is ~10-100x the underdoped-cuprate carrier density. Falsified if the only altermagnets with usable spin splitting are low-carrier insulators/semimetals (MnTe-class), forcing D_s^eff back below the cuprate wall.
beats_phase_stiffness_ceiling: Directly attacks the wall's ROOT. The 134-164K ceiling is cuprate-specific in ONE respect the survey under-weighted: cuprate low D_s comes from being a doped Mott insulator (carriers are scarce because you must dope away from half-filling). An altermagnet's compensation is a SYMMETRY property of the lattice, not a consequence of carrier starvation — so a metallic altermagnet could have BOTH a spin-fluctuation-class pairing vertex AND a high-carrier-density charge phase stiffness an order of magnitude above the cuprate value. T_BKT = (pi/2)D_s then sits well above 164K if the pairing vertex (seed 1) survives.
novelty: The frozen 'phase-stiffness wall is UNIVERSAL' verdict (research-geometric-stiffness file) was reached on cuprate + flat-band geometric hosts, ALL of which are intrinsically low-D_s (doped-Mott or flat-band-zero-velocity). A high-carrier metallic compensated magnet is a category that wall-classification never tested — orthogonal to both the magnon family and the flat-band/multilayer D_s levers.

name: ANISOTROPY-LOCKED GLUE (crystal-symmetry-protected pairing immune to order melting)
basis: GROUNDED concept. Altermagnetic spin splitting is protected by a SPIN-GROUP symmetry that combines a real-space rotation with a spin rotation (Smejkal et al. 2022; Liu/Yang spin-space-group classification, Nature 626, 517 (2024), arXiv:2307.10371). SPECULATIVE application: because the splitting is locked to a crystallographic rotation rather than to the magnitude of the local moment, thermal melting of the moment amplitude (which kills a normal magnon glue at T_N) degrades the altermagnet vertex MUCH more slowly than it degrades a Goldstone-magnon vertex — the symmetry survives even as <S> shrinks, as long as short-range altermagnetic correlations persist.
falsifiable_prediction: In a finite-T QMC/DMFT or classical-MC-plus-RPA study of an altermagnet Hubbard model, the pairing vertex's effective coupling lambda_pair(T) retains >50% of its T=0 value up to T ~ 0.7 T_N, whereas a control AFM-magnon vertex on the same lattice falls below 50% by T ~ 0.3 T_N. Falsified if lambda_pair(T) tracks <S>^2 identically to the AFM control (meaning the symmetry protection gives no thermal robustness).
beats_phase_stiffness_ceiling: The ceiling is partly a finite-T fluctuation/competing-order wall: as T rises toward the would-be T_c, the magnetic order's amplitude fluctuations both supply the glue AND destroy the coherence. If the altermagnet vertex is symmetry-locked and survives moment-amplitude fluctuations, the glue stays strong at temperatures where a Goldstone-magnon glue has already self-destructed — pushing the pairing temperature above the ~164K point where the magnon family thermally collapses, without needing a larger boson energy.
novelty: Reframes the campaign's deep law: instead of 'the fluctuation glues, the order traps,' an altermagnet offers glue carried by a SYMMETRY (anisotropy) that is partially decoupled from the order's amplitude — a structurally new escape from the order-trap that no surveyed family (all of which tie glue strength to order amplitude) possesses.

name: Stiffness-Native Inverse Design (the optimizer maximizes D_s, not the gap)
basis: GROUNDED-METHOD on SPECULATIVE-TARGET. Differentiable-DFT / autodiff electronic-structure (e.g. JAX-DFT, DFTK.jl adjoint, FermiNet/DeepQMC gradients) lets you backprop through a band structure to host parameters. Every surveyed inverse-design effort optimizes a PROXY for pairing — lambda*omega, ⟨g⟩·U, flat-band tr g. The campaign's own research (research-glue-multilayer-and-spinfluct-ceiling) shows the wall is NOT pairing: it is the BKT phase-stiffness J = (hbar^2/4e^2)·(n_s/m*)·d. So make the OBJECTIVE the BKT stiffness functional T_BKT ∝ D_s itself — a quantity computable in-silico via the Kubo superfluid-weight / quantum-geometric formula (Peotta-Törmä, Nat.Commun.6,8944 2015) — and let the search find the host that maximizes n_s/m* AND the geometric contribution D_s^geom SIMULTANEOUSLY, under the hard constraint of zero competing order.
falsifiable_prediction: If you run multi-objective Bayesian/gradient optimization with objective = computed BKT stiffness (Kubo+geometric) and constraint = no static order parameter within k_BT of the SC gap, the Pareto front of D_s over the screened host space saturates at T_BKT < 200 K for ALL no-competing-order solutions — i.e. the optimizer cannot find ANY corner where geometric+conventional stiffness jointly exceed the 164 K wall without re-introducing a competing order. (Falsified if even one stable, dynamically-stable, order-free host yields computed T_BKT > 200 K.)
beats_phase_stiffness_ceiling: By optimizing the stiffness functional DIRECTLY rather than a pairing proxy, the search explores the geometric contribution D_s^geom (which adds to n_s/m* and is NOT bounded by the spin-fluctuation pairing scale) as a free knob — the conjecture is that a machine can find a host where the geometric stiffness lifts the BKT temperature above the magnon-pairing ceiling because BKT T is set by D_s, not by the boson energy. The wall is only ~134-164 K for hosts humans picked by pairing strength; the optimizer searches the orthogonal stiffness axis the human never optimized.
novelty: Surveyed flat-band/quantum-geometry work uses tr g as a FIXED hand-picked target (CoSn, ∫tr g=2.86) and optimizes pairing. This inverts the loss: the human never let an optimizer treat the phase-stiffness wall itself as the differentiable objective. It is the only seed where the 134-164 K ceiling is the OPTIMIZATION TARGET, not an after-the-fact diagnosis.

name: Adversarial Trap-Hunter (a discriminator that learns 'order traps' and steers the generator around them)
basis: SPECULATIVE architecture built on a REAL law. The campaign's deepest finding is 'the fluctuation glues, the order traps' — every strong-glue host the search finds is sabotaged by a competing static order (EI-family DEAD, 1T-TiSe2 IS its CDW, pseudogap traps cuprates). Frame this as a GAN/adversarial active-learning loop: a GENERATOR (diffusion/VAE over crystal graphs, cf. CDVAE, Xie et al. ICLR 2022, arXiv:2110.06197) proposes hosts; a learned DISCRIMINATOR/critic — trained on DFT+DMFT susceptibility data to predict the leading competing-order instability (CDW/SDW/PDW) and its T_order — penalizes any host whose T_order > T_SC. The adversary's job is to FIND the trap; the generator's job is to evade it. Convergence = a host where the only diverging susceptibility is the SC channel.
falsifiable_prediction: Train the discriminator on the known trapped hosts (Ta2NiSe5, 1T-TiSe2, optimally-doped cuprate) to predict leading-instability T_order from structure. Prediction: across the generator's full reachable manifold of dynamically-stable order-free hosts, the SC susceptibility and the strongest competing-order susceptibility are LINEARLY CORRELATED with slope ~1 (a conservation/sum-rule: the same nesting/DOS that feeds SC feeds the trap). Falsified if the generator finds a stable host where dχ_SC/dλ is large while ALL competing dχ_order/dλ stay flat — i.e. the trap and the glue genuinely decouple.
beats_phase_stiffness_ceiling: It does NOT try to out-muscle the stiffness wall by brute pairing; it tests whether the wall is a TRADE-OFF (glue strength conserved against trap strength) or genuinely evadable. If the adversary fails to find ANY decoupled host, that is a constructive proof the 134-164 K ceiling is a conservation law, not a search failure — a publishable closed-negative. If it succeeds, the decoupled host has high stiffness WITHOUT the competing order that normally pins T_c at the wall.
novelty: Surveyed work treats competing order as a binary disqualifier checked per-candidate after the fact. No surveyed family makes the trap a LEARNED adversary inside the generative loop, nor tests the glue-vs-trap conservation hypothesis. It operationalizes the campaign's own meta-law 'order traps' as a discriminator network.

name: Out-of-Distribution Extrapolation Probe (does the surrogate hallucinate a >293 K host, and is the hallucination physical?)
basis: GROUNDED-as-meta-experiment. A GNN surrogate (cf. M3GNet/MACE/Open-Catalyst-class universal interatomic + a property head trained on the ~entire computed-Tc literature) interpolates within its training distribution. Every published high-Tc host (which the campaign notes is novelty=0, all already published) lies BELOW the wall. The probe: run generative inverse design EXPLICITLY into the extrapolation regime (target T_c=293 K, far outside training support) and ask not 'what host?' but 'WHAT FEATURE does the surrogate believe must change to cross the wall?' — via gradient saliency / counterfactual attribution on the surrogate. This is a real ML diagnostic (integrated gradients, Sundararajan et al. 2017) applied as a physics-hypothesis generator.
falsifiable_prediction: When the surrogate is forced to extrapolate to 293 K, its saliency map will point at ONE dominant feature direction. Falsifiable prediction: that direction will be a stiffness/superfluid-density feature (n_s, dimensionality, layer count) NOT a boson-energy feature (omega, lambda) — i.e. the trained-on-reality surrogate independently 'rediscovers' that the wall is phase-stiffness-limited. Falsified if the saliency instead points at pairing-boson energy (which would mean the literature regression sees the wall as a glue-energy wall, contradicting Emery-Kivelson).
beats_phase_stiffness_ceiling: It does not beat the wall directly — it extracts, from the collective fit to all known materials, the MINIMAL feature-change the data itself implies is needed to cross 293 K. If that change is a physically-attainable stiffness knob (e.g. a specific n_s/m* uplift) the surrogate has handed a falsifiable target; if the required extrapolation is non-physical (negative effective mass, unattainable n_s), that is evidence the wall is fundamental. Either way the wall is characterized, not assumed.
novelty: Surveyed families propose mechanisms; this seed uses the trained surrogate's OWN extrapolation gradient as the hypothesis source — a machine-discovered claim about which lever the aggregate data says is binding. Orthogonal to all hand-derived mechanisms (flat-band, multilayer, demand-relaxation) because the lever is read OUT of a model, not put in.

name: Constraint-Manifold Walker (search the disorder/strain/composition continuum, not the discrete crystal database)
basis: SPECULATIVE. Standard inverse design (CDVAE, GNoME, Merchant et al. Nature 2023) searches DISCRETE stoichiometric crystals. But the phase-stiffness knob D_s ∝ n_s/m* is tunable on a CONTINUUM via doping, epitaxial strain, and substitutional disorder — none of which live cleanly in a crystal-graph generator. Reformulate as Bayesian active learning over a CONTINUOUS design vector (strain tensor εij, carrier density n, disorder correlation length ξ) with the acquisition function targeting the geometric+conventional D_s computed by a strained/disordered tight-binding+Kubo surrogate. The optimizer walks the continuous constraint manifold between known hosts rather than jumping between database entries.
falsifiable_prediction: Active-learning over (strain, doping, disorder) for the campaign's strongest no-exotic-glue host (Ta2NiSe5-class, H_023 demand-relaxation candidate) will identify a continuous-parameter optimum. Prediction: the D_s-maximizing point requires an epitaxial strain that simultaneously SOFTENS the competing-order gap — and the achievable D_s at the order-free boundary still caps T_BKT below ~200 K. Falsified if any (ε, n, ξ) point on the dynamically-stable, order-free manifold yields computed T_BKT > 250 K.
beats_phase_stiffness_ceiling: Strain and disorder move n_s/m* and the geometric stiffness continuously and can, in principle, push D_s past where any discrete host sits — the discrete database may simply not CONTAIN the optimal strained/doped point. The hypothesis is that the 134-164 K wall is an artifact of sampling only equilibrium stoichiometric crystals; the continuous manifold between them might host a higher-stiffness corner the discrete search structurally cannot reach.
novelty: All surveyed inverse-design-adjacent work (and the GNoME/CDVAE lineage) searches discrete crystals; the multilayer/3D/demand-relaxation levers are applied to fixed hosts. No surveyed family runs the optimizer over the CONTINUOUS strain-doping-disorder manifold with D_s as the acquisition target. It is the only seed treating the inter-material continuum, not the material list, as the search space.

name: AB-CAGE BREATHER — flux-detuned Aharonov-Bohm caging that LIFTS the cage just enough to pay stiffness
axis: BIC/CLS flat band via destructive interference: Aharonov-Bohm caging on a rhombic/diamond/sawtooth lattice tuned by a synthetic flux NEAR (not exactly at) the full-caging value pi.
basis: Aharonov-Bohm caging is a real, established mechanism: Vidal, Mosseri, Doucot PRL 81, 5888 (1998) (dice/T3 lattice full localization at flux = half-quantum); realized in superconducting/photonic/cold-atom arrays (e.g. Kang et al. flux-caging in circuit lattices; Mukherjee et al. Nat.Commun. AB caging photonics). The CLS exists ONLY at the exact caging flux. SPECULATIVE extension: detuning the synthetic flux by delta away from the caging point gives the flat band a TUNABLE curvature epsilon(delta) DECOUPLED from the on-site/interaction gap — so band-velocity stiffness and pairing gap become two independent knobs (flux delta vs |U|), unlike the moiré case where both ride the single bandgap.
falsifiable_prediction: In an attractive-Hubbard ED/DMRG on a rhombic AB-caging chain, the BKT superfluid stiffness D_s, swept versus flux-detuning delta at FIXED |U|, will show a maximum at a NONZERO delta* (a finite curvature that out-competes both the delta=0 caged limit, where D_s is purely geometric/tiny, and the large-delta dispersive limit). If D_s(delta) is monotone decreasing from delta=0, the seed is falsified — the flux knob buys no stiffness independent of |U|.
beats_phase_stiffness_ceiling: The frozen wall is D_s ∝ |U|·⟨g⟩ with large ⟨g⟩ forcing small gap forcing small |U|. AB caging breaks the gap-vs-metric coupling: the cage is enforced by FLUX (a phase), not by a small bandgap, so |U| can stay large (gap set by lattice hopping t, which is O(eV), not by an avoided crossing). Detuning then adds CONVENTIONAL band-velocity stiffness on top of the geometric piece WITHOUT shrinking |U|, so the product |U|·(geom+velocity) is no longer pinned small. Total D_s can exceed the geometric-only ceiling that capped MATBG at ~1 K.
novelty: Surveyed flat-band route was Peotta-Tormae geometric stiffness on moiré/CoSn bands, found stiffness-limited because metric and gap are coupled. AB caging is a DIFFERENT flat-band origin (flux phase, not band touching) with an independent flux knob; orthogonal to moiré-of-moiré (no twist), to O2 disorder (perfectly clean), and to CoSn geometric stiffness (which has no flux tuning).

name: CLS-PHONON HYBRID GLUE — using the compact localized state's open continuum channel as a resonance reservoir (Friedrich-Wintgen BIC)
axis: Bound-state-in-continuum flat band: a Friedrich-Wintgen BIC formed when two resonance channels destructively interfere, leaving a flat (zero-width) band embedded IN a dispersive continuum that still carries current.
basis: Friedrich & Wintgen, Phys. Rev. A 32, 3231 (1985) (interfering-resonance BIC); Hsu et al. Nat.Rev.Mater. 1, 16048 (2016) (BIC review). REAL: a BIC is a localized state degenerate with and embedded inside a propagating continuum — uniquely, the SAME energy hosts BOTH a flat (pairing-favorable) and a dispersive (stiffness-carrying) channel. SPECULATIVE: pair electrons in the flat BIC branch (high DOS -> strong pairing) while the coexisting continuum band at the same energy provides the phase stiffness, the two coupled by the same Coulomb |U|. The pairs need not live entirely in the flat band.
falsifiable_prediction: A two-channel (flat-BIC + continuum) attractive lattice model will show, in ED, that the pair condensate weight resides in the flat branch while D_s scales with the CONTINUUM bandwidth W_cont, giving Tc-stiffness that grows with W_cont at fixed pairing gap. Falsified if D_s is independent of W_cont (i.e. the continuum does not donate stiffness to the BIC-paired condensate).
beats_phase_stiffness_ceiling: The wall assumes pairs AND phase coherence both live in the flat band, so D_s is bounded by the flat-band geometric term. A BIC co-locates a dispersive continuum AT THE PAIRING ENERGY. If pairing draws DOS from the flat branch but coherence rides the continuum's W_cont (O(eV)), then D_s ~ |U|·n_pair·W_cont-derived rather than |U|·⟨g⟩(flat) — decoupling pairing strength from stiffness and lifting the ~134 K wall set by stiffness-limited flat-band condensates.
novelty: All surveyed flat-band seeds (CoSn, moiré, disorder O2) put the whole condensate in ONE flat band -> stiffness-limited. The BIC is the only construction where a flat and a dispersive band are forced to share one energy by destructive interference; nothing surveyed exploits a continuum-coexisting flat band. Orthogonal to the exciton/plasmon glue families (this is geometry, not a boson).

name: LINE-GRAPH SATELLITE LADDER — stacking the dispersive partner band of a line-graph flat band to harvest its stiffness
axis: Line-graph/Lieb/kagome compact-localized-state flat band, but engineering the COUPLING to the line graph's mandatory dispersive bands rather than isolating the flat band.
basis: REAL: every line-graph flat band (kagome = line graph of honeycomb; checkerboard = line graph of square) comes with dispersive partner bands touching it quadratically — Sutherland PRB 34, 5208 (1986); Mielke J.Phys.A 24, L73 (1991) (line-graph flat-band ferromagnetism/SC). The CLS basis is exactly known. SPECULATIVE: introduce an interlayer/sublattice coupling lambda that converts the quadratic band-touching into a NARROW but FINITE-velocity hybridized band whose Berry curvature is concentrated (high ⟨g⟩) yet whose group velocity is nonzero — a 'satellite' band engineered to maximize the |U|·(⟨g⟩ + v^2/W) sum.
falsifiable_prediction: Sweeping the touching-gap lambda in an attractive line-graph (checkerboard) Hubbard ED, the BKT D_s will be NON-MONOTONIC with a peak at finite lambda* where geometric and velocity contributions both nonzero — and the peak D_s will exceed the isolated-flat-band (lambda=0) value by >2x. Falsified if isolated flat band (lambda=0) already maximizes D_s.
beats_phase_stiffness_ceiling: The stiffness wall comes from forcing the condensate into the ISOLATED flat band (gap to dispersive partner kills velocity). Line graphs uniquely guarantee a dispersive partner DEGENERATE at the touching point — so a tiny lambda hybridizes them into a band with both finite ⟨g⟩ and finite v at essentially no |U| cost (lambda sets velocity, not the Hubbard gap). The D_s product gains a velocity term the pure-flat route lacks, plausibly clearing the ~1-8 K measured flat-band ceiling without trading away |U|.
novelty: The campaign's CoSn work treated the flat band as the asset and the dispersive bands as contamination. This inverts it: the mandatory line-graph partner band is the stiffness DONOR. Orthogonal to moiré (line graphs are atomic-scale, no twist), to O2 disorder, and to the geometric-stiffness seed (which discarded the partner band).

name: INTERFERENCE-PROTECTED PAIR BICs — a two-particle bound state in the continuum that cannot decay into single-particle channels
axis: Bound-state-in-continuum applied to the TWO-PARTICLE (pair) sector: a Cooper-like bound pair whose center-of-mass momentum sits in a destructive-interference node so it cannot dissociate into the single-particle continuum.
basis: REAL building blocks: two-particle BICs / interaction-induced BICs exist — Zhang et al. PRL 109, 116405 (2012) and Della Valle/Longhi (two-boson BIC in Hubbard dimers); doublon BICs in Hubbard chains (Gorlach & Poddubny PRA 95, 053604). SPECULATIVE synthesis: engineer a lattice where the PAIR dispersion has a flat, interference-protected branch (pair-BIC) while single particles remain dispersive — so the pairing gap and single-particle stiffness are carried by DIFFERENT sectors entirely.
falsifiable_prediction: In an attractive-Hubbard ED on a lattice with a designed pair-BIC (e.g. cross-stitch or diamond chain at the caging point), the two-particle spectrum will show a flat pair band that is robust (long-lived, no broadening) inside the two-particle continuum, AND the resulting condensate D_s will track the SINGLE-particle bandwidth, not the pair-band flatness. Falsified if the pair-BIC broadens (acquires finite width -> not a true BIC) or if D_s collapses to the flat-pair-band scale.
beats_phase_stiffness_ceiling: The deep law 'fluctuation glues, order traps' and the stiffness wall both assume ONE band sets pairing and coherence. A pair-BIC physically SEPARATES the sectors: pairs are bound and immobile-in-energy (deep binding, high Tc-pairing) by interference, but single-particle (and hence collective phase) transport stays dispersive, so phase stiffness is NOT tied to a flat band. This is a mechanism to get strong pairing without the flat-band stiffness penalty that capped every measured flat-band SC below 8 K.
novelty: Every surveyed flat-band route flattens the SINGLE-particle band. No surveyed seed flattens the PAIR band while keeping single particles dispersive. This is orthogonal to all five boson-glue families (binding is interference, not a boson) and to topology/fractionalization (the BIC is a real-space interference node, not a topological invariant).

name: Dipole-Locked Condensate (the pair that flows, the charge that cannot)
basis: Dipole-symmetry / center-of-mass conservation: in a dipole-conserving system a single charge is an immobile fracton, but a charge-dipole (= a true two-electron bound state preserving total dipole moment) moves freely. Real anchors: 'Dipole-Obstructed Cooper Pairing: Theory and Application to j=3/2 Superconductors' (arXiv:2405.00091), 'Fractons from a liquid of singlet pairs' (arXiv:2012.11708), and the dipole-conserving Bose-Hubbard mobility result that single particles are frozen while pairs co-move (arXiv:2511.02343, arXiv:2206.12877). SPECULATIVE step: engineer the superconducting carrier to BE the mobile dipole while the unpaired electron is the frozen fracton, so pair-breaking is not just energetically costly (the gap) but KINEMATICALLY forbidden — a broken pair must shed dipole charge that has nowhere to go.
falsifiable_prediction: In a tight-binding lattice with an explicit dipole-conserving correlated-hop term (pair hop t_pair allowed, single hop t_1 forbidden by the symmetry), the single-particle spectral weight at the Fermi level is gapped/immobile while the pair (dipole) propagator remains gapless, AND the effective pair-breaking activation energy measured from the pair-susceptibility scales with the dipole-violation coupling, not with the BCS gap. If a finite single-particle mobility survives in the exact-diagonalization spectrum when t_1=0, the kinematic-protection claim is false.
beats_phase_stiffness_ceiling: The Emery-Kivelson ~134-164K wall is a statement about a conventional phase Goldstone whose superfluid stiffness D_s sets Tc via BKT/3D-XY. Here the rigidity against phase-disordering is not Goldstone stiffness at all — it is a CONSERVATION LAW: thermally exciting the phase-disordering excitation (an unbound single charge) requires creating an isolated fracton, which costs not k_B*T_theta-stiffness but the dipole-symmetry-breaking scale. Tc is then bounded by the dipole-conservation scale (a microscopic interaction energy, ~eV) rather than by the inter-pair phase stiffness, decoupling Tc from the D_s ceiling.
novelty: The surveyed campaign closed topology/fractionalization as an INDEPENDENT statistical-rigidity lever (anyon/CS braiding). This is orthogonal: rigidity here is kinematic (multipole conservation), and crucially it acts on the PAIR-BREAKING channel, not the phase-winding channel — a different failure mode than every glue family (which all fight pair formation) and different from the anyon seeds (which fight phase slips via statistics).

name: Tkachenko Ceiling-Breaker (quadratic Goldstone, no BKT wall)
basis: Fractonic superfluids spontaneously break a multipole (dipole) symmetry, producing a Goldstone with QUADRATIC dispersion (omega ~ q^2, Tkachenko/Lifshitz-scalar type) rather than the linear omega ~ q of a normal superfluid. Real anchors: 'Fractonic superfluids II/III' (arXiv:2010.03261, 2412.10280), 'Ideal fracton superfluids' (arXiv:2304.09596), 'On quantum melting of superfluid vortex crystals: Lifshitz scalar to dual gravity' (SciPost Phys 17,164 / arXiv:2310.13741), and 'Goldstone bosons & fluctuating hydrodynamics with dipole and momentum conservation' (arXiv:2206... / RG 366984060). The Emery-Kivelson stiffness-wall and the entire D_s-sets-Tc logic assume a LINEAR phase mode and a BKT/3D-XY transition; a quadratic Goldstone has a different phase-space measure for thermal fluctuations and a different (Lifshitz, z=2) finite-T phase-ordering criterion.
falsifiable_prediction: A 2D/quasi-2D fractonic-superfluid lattice model with an enforced quadratic phase mode will show a finite-T ordering temperature whose ratio Tc / D_eff (D_eff = the q^2 coefficient / Lifshitz rigidity) EXCEEDS the universal BKT bound Tc <= (pi/2) D_s that caps linear-Goldstone superfluids — i.e. the standard stiffness-ceiling inequality is violated because the wrong (linear) criterion was applied. If a classical/quantum Monte Carlo of the z=2 model reproduces the BKT bound exactly, the seed is falsified.
beats_phase_stiffness_ceiling: The 134-164K number is the saturation of a LINEAR-Goldstone BKT/3D-XY estimate Tc ~ D_s. A z=2 (quadratic) Goldstone changes the infrared phase-fluctuation integral: thermal phase fluctuations <delta-theta^2> ~ integral d^dq T/(rho q^4) instead of T/(rho q^2), shifting the lower critical dimension and the Tc-vs-rigidity relation. The same microscopic rigidity rho can then support order at a higher T than the linear formula predicts — the ceiling is an artifact of assuming z=1, not a true energy wall.
novelty: Directly attacks the campaign's stated deep law (the spin-fluctuation phase-stiffness ceiling) at its hidden ASSUMPTION — that the phase Goldstone is linear. No surveyed family questions the dispersion of the order-parameter Goldstone itself; flat-band/quantum-geometry boosts D_s but still inside the z=1 BKT formula. This changes the universality class, not the magnitude of D_s.

name: Fractal-Membrane Phase Coherence (sub-extensive defect cost)
basis: In type-I/type-II fracton orders (X-cube, Haah's code), the phase-disordering topological defects are created only at CORNERS of membrane- or fractal-shaped operators, so an isolated phase slip costs an energy that grows with system size / cannot be created locally; ground-state degeneracy is sub-extensive. Real anchors: Haah's code (Haah 2011, PRA 83, 042330), X-cube model (Vijay-Haah-Fu, arXiv:1603.04442), 'Fracton Topological Order at Finite Temperature' (arXiv:2109.06887), and 'Chiral Topological Elasticity and Fracton Order' (arXiv:1712.06600). SPECULATIVE step: identify a superconducting order parameter whose vortex (the phase-disordering object that kills BKT/3D-XY coherence) inherits fracton mobility constraints, so a free vortex requires a membrane operator and is suppressed by a sub-extensive (rather than logarithmic) energy.
falsifiable_prediction: In a coupled-layer / X-cube-coupled superconductor model, the free-vortex creation energy scales with linear system size L (membrane-bounded) instead of ln L (conventional 2D vortex). Consequently the vortex-unbinding (coherence-destroying) temperature does NOT saturate at the single-layer BKT value but grows with the membrane-coupling scale. If exact-diagonalization / tensor-network estimates of the lowest vortex-pair gap stay ~ln L, the fracton-protection of coherence is falsified.
beats_phase_stiffness_ceiling: The ceiling is set by how cheaply phase coherence is destroyed (vortex proliferation at T ~ D_s). If the coherence-killing defect is mobility-restricted (needs a membrane, cost ~L), vortex proliferation is parametrically suppressed and the coherence temperature is bounded by the membrane/inter-layer coupling, not by the in-plane stiffness. The wall moves from a 2D-XY stiffness to a 3D sub-extensive defect cost.
novelty: Distinct from the surveyed multilayer-D_s boost and 3D-lever: those raise the SAME stiffness quantity in a thicker stack (still linear-Goldstone, log-vortex). This instead changes the TOPOLOGY/MOBILITY of the disordering defect itself (membrane vs point), a kinematic suppression of vortices rather than a stiffness increase. Orthogonal to the closed anyon/CS seeds (point anyons are mobile; fractons are not).

name: Partially-Massless Higgs (rank-2 gauge eats the phase)
basis: When a rank-2 symmetric-tensor (higher-rank U(1)) gauge field undergoes a Higgs/Meissner mechanism by a fracton condensate, the resulting superconductor is qualitatively different from a Maxwell-Higgs one — the photon becomes 'partially massless' and the Meissner/screening response is anisotropic and momentum-structured. Real anchors: 'A Partially Massless Superconductor' (arXiv:2507.15932), 'The Higgs Mechanism in Higher-Rank Symmetric U(1) Gauge Theories' (arXiv:1802.10099), 'Higgs mechanism of rank-2 gauge theory and fracton topological order' (Bulmash-Barkeshli line). SPECULATIVE step: a real material whose emergent low-energy gauge sector is rank-2 (e.g. from a dipole-conserving correlated-electron fluid) would screen magnetic fields and rigidify its phase via a tensor London equation, with a stiffness controlled by the tensor gauge coupling rather than the scalar superfluid density.
falsifiable_prediction: The London/Meissner response of a rank-2 Higgs superconductor is a tensor (anisotropic, with a q-dependent penetration depth lambda(q) that does NOT go to a constant as q->0 in the partially-massless direction). In-silico: computing the current-current (Kubo) response of a dipole-conserving lattice superconductor yields a kernel K_ij(q) with a higher-rank (q-structured) form, not the scalar K(q->0)=1/lambda^2. If the computed Meissner kernel reduces to the ordinary scalar London form, the rank-2/partially-massless claim is false.
beats_phase_stiffness_ceiling: The campaign's Kubo-stiffness pipeline measures a SCALAR D_s and bounds Tc by it. A rank-2 Higgs phase has a TENSOR rigidity whose largest eigenvalue (the tensor gauge coupling) can exceed the scalar superfluid-density stiffness that caps conventional Tc — the relevant rigidity for phase coherence is the gauge-sector tensor stiffness, a different and potentially larger quantity than the carrier D_s the Emery-Kivelson wall constrains.
novelty: Reframes the very observable the campaign's stiffness wall is built on (scalar Kubo D_s) as the q->0 scalar truncation of a tensor response. No surveyed family (boson glues, flat-band geometry, anyon statistics) touches the RANK of the screening response; this says the 134-164K ceiling may be measuring the wrong (scalar) component of a tensor stiffness. Directly testable inside the existing kubo_stiffness tool by generalizing it to a tensor kernel.

name: Amperean Forge — transverse-gauge current-current glue (no boson, no cavity)
axis: Momentum-space pairing geometry: current-current (Amperean) attraction mediated by the metal's OWN transverse gauge response, not an exchanged boson
basis: GROUNDED concept. Amperean pairing: Lee, 'Amperean pairing and the pseudogap phase of cuprates' (Phys. Rev. X 4, 031017, 2014); Sung-Sik Lee / Patrick Lee on transverse gauge fields of a spinon Fermi surface (U(1) spin liquid) giving a current-current interaction that is ATTRACTIVE for pairs of parallel-moving (same-side-of-FS) electrons — a finite-Q condensate. The glue is the unscreened transverse (Ampere-law) part of an emergent or real gauge field; longitudinal Coulomb is screened, transverse is NOT. This is structurally distinct from all 5 surveyed boson-glue families (it is not a propagating boson with a frequency ceiling Omega — it is the gauge KERNEL of the metal).
falsifiable_prediction: In an RPA/ED solver, the BCS-like instability driven ONLY by the transverse current-current kernel chi_JJ(q,w) (Coulomb-longitudinal removed) of a spinon-Fermi-surface or near-ferromagnetic metal yields a finite-Q pair susceptibility whose leading eigenvalue, scaled to T_c, exceeds the Q=0 spin-fluctuation eigenvalue for the SAME interaction strength. If the transverse-kernel eigenvalue is <= the Q=0 one at all fillings, the family is closed.
beats_phase_stiffness_ceiling: The Emery-Kivelson ~134-164K ceiling is a Q=0 phase-stiffness wall set by the longitudinal superfluid density of a uniform condensate. A current-current (Amperean) condensate's phase mode couples to the TRANSVERSE gauge sector; its rigidity is governed by the diamagnetic/Landau-damping kernel of the same gauge field rather than the carrier superfluid density n_s/m. Because the transverse kernel is unscreened and set by the FULL Fermi-surface current response (not the dilute pair density), the stiffness scale can be parametrically decoupled from n_s — the ceiling derivation assumes a longitudinal stiffness it never has.
novelty: Every surveyed family (phonon/exciton/plasmon/magnon, flat-band geometry, 3D/multilayer) pairs at Q=0 with a longitudinal boson or static metric. Amperean glue is the transverse gauge KERNEL itself — no exchanged boson, no cavity, finite-Q. Orthogonal to the 'fluctuation glues / order traps' law because the glue is not an order-parameter fluctuation at all.

name: FFLO Stiffness Bypass — finite-Q phase rigidity from the depairing tensor, not n_s
axis: Momentum-space pairing geometry: FFLO/LOFF finite-momentum (Q!=0) condensate where the relevant phase stiffness is the FFLO modulation stiffness, a different tensor from the Q=0 superfluid weight
basis: GROUNDED concept. Fulde-Ferrell (Phys. Rev. 135, A550, 1964) and Larkin-Ovchinnikov (1965): under a Fermi-surface mismatch (Zeeman, valley, or spin-imbalance) the leading instability is to a pair carrying center-of-mass momentum Q ~ 2*delta_k_F. The order parameter Delta(r) ~ cos(Q.r) is spatially modulated. SPECULATIVE extension: the modulation-direction phase stiffness of an LO state is set by the gradient term in the modulated-GL functional, which scales with the FS-mismatch energy, NOT with the dilute uniform superfluid density — so the Emery-Kivelson Q=0 stiffness bound is the wrong functional to evaluate.
falsifiable_prediction: For a 2-band/valley-imbalanced lattice model with controllable mismatch delta, the GL stiffness tensor of the LO (cos Q.r) saddle, computed from the ED/BdG free-energy curvature, has a component along Q whose Kelvin-equivalent scale grows with delta and can exceed (pi/2)*D_s^{Q=0}. If the LO modulation stiffness is always <= the Q=0 superfluid stiffness across the mismatch range, the bypass is closed.
beats_phase_stiffness_ceiling: The 134-164K wall is (pi/2)*D_s evaluated for the UNIFORM Q=0 condensate, which for low-carrier flat-band/cuprate hosts is tiny. An LO condensate has TWO phase-like modes: the U(1) phase AND the sliding phase of the cos(Q.r) modulation. The sliding-mode stiffness is set by the mismatch/bandwidth energy (a large scale), and BKT/melting of the modulated order can be governed by that larger stiffness, lifting T_c above the Q=0 n_s-limited ceiling.
novelty: All surveyed families and the deep law are Q=0. FFLO is intrinsically finite-Q and brings a second stiffness tensor the ceiling derivation never considers. Orthogonal to magnon/spin-fluctuation because the mechanism is the mismatch geometry, not a glue boson.

name: PDW Commensurate Lock — pair-density-wave pinned to the lattice for fluctuation-proof rigidity
axis: Momentum-space pairing geometry: a pair-density-wave (finite-Q Cooper condensate) commensurately locked to the lattice so its phase is pinned, converting soft phase fluctuations into a gapped amplitudon
basis: GROUNDED + SPECULATIVE. PDW as a distinct condensate: Agterberg, Davis, Fradkin et al., 'The Physics of Pair-Density Waves' (Annu. Rev. Condens. Matter Phys. 11, 231, 2020); Berg-Fradkin-Kivelson striped-SC. SPECULATIVE lever: choose Q commensurate (Q = G/n) so the modulation locks to the lattice via an Umklapp/pinning term ~ cos(n*theta); this gaps the sliding Goldstone mode. The campaign's deep law says 'the order traps' because a uniform order kills the fluctuation glue — but a PINNED finite-Q order has NO soft phase channel to trap into, so the order-trap may not bite the same way.
falsifiable_prediction: In a lattice ED/BdG model with a commensurate finite-Q pairing channel, adding a lattice-commensurate Umklapp pinning term opens a phase gap (amplitudon-only spectrum) and raises the Kosterlitz-Thouless/order-melting temperature above the unpinned (incommensurate) value by a factor set by the pinning strength. If the pinning gap leaves T_c unchanged or lowers it (because pinning also suppresses the pairing eigenvalue), the family is closed.
beats_phase_stiffness_ceiling: Emery-Kivelson assumes a gapless U(1) phase whose thermal proliferation (vortices/phase slips) at scale (pi/2)D_s destroys order. A commensurately pinned PDW replaces the gapless phase Goldstone with a GAPPED phason; vortex proliferation costs the pinning gap, not just the small n_s stiffness — so the melting temperature is set by max(stiffness-scale, pinning-gap), potentially above 164K even at low n_s.
novelty: Surveyed families fight the stiffness ceiling by raising D_s (geometry/3D/multilayer) — all still gapless-phase Q=0. This instead REMOVES the soft phase channel via commensurate lattice pinning of a finite-Q condensate. Directly attacks the 'order traps' clause of the deep law from the momentum-space side.

name: Stiffness Donor Co-Tenancy (the Leggett-locked composite condensate)
basis: Suhl-Matthias-Walker two-band BCS (Phys.Rev.Lett.3,552,1959) + Leggett interband phase mode (Leggett, Prog.Theor.Phys.36,901,1966). In a genuine two-band condensate the TOTAL superfluid weight is additive across bands, D_s^tot = D_s^flat + D_s^deep, and the interband Josephson-like coupling g_12 phase-locks the two order parameters so they share ONE global phase. The deep high-velocity band carries a LARGE conventional (band-velocity) stiffness; the flat band supplies the large DOS that sets the pairing scale. Measured precedent for additivity: MgB2 (σ and π bands, Tc=39K) and iron-pnictide s±. SPECULATIVE part = engineering the DOS-band flat and the donor-band fast in the SAME crystal at the same E_F.
falsifiable_prediction: Build a 2-orbital tight-binding model (flat band + dispersive band degenerate at E_F, interband attraction g_12) and solve self-consistent two-band BdG + Kubo. Prediction: the composite T_BKT computed from D_s^tot = D_s^flat + D_s^deep exceeds the flat-band-alone T_BKT by a factor >2 when the deep-band Fermi velocity v_F satisfies (1/2)N_deep v_F^2 > (2/π)k_B(134K). FALSIFIED if, when the interband coupling g_12 is turned on, the Leggett mode goes soft (mass^2 -> 0) and the composite stiffness collapses back to min(band stiffnesses) rather than summing.
beats_phase_stiffness_ceiling: The 134-164K ceiling was measured/frozen on systems where ONE band does both jobs, so Tc=min(pairing, stiffness) is pinned by whichever band is the bottleneck. Here the jobs are SPLIT across k-space: the flat band's job is only pairing (its tiny stiffness no longer gates Tc), and the deep band donates a conventional band-velocity stiffness that can be >>7.4 meV. Tc is then re-pinned to min(flat-band pairing ~300meV, deep-band donated stiffness), and the deep band's stiffness is a tunable v_F^2*N knob NOT bounded by the cuprate value. The campaign's own finding that flat-band geometric D_s is 20-90x too small is sidestepped because the stiffness is borrowed, not geometric.
novelty: The surveyed multilayer D_s boost splits the two jobs SPATIALLY (different layers, with electron-opacity/tunneling loss already measured as the killer). Here both jobs live in ONE crystal at different k, so there is NO interlayer tunneling barrier to lose stiffness across — the bands are co-resident and the phase lock is intrinsic (Leggett), not a proximity sandwich. Orthogonal to all 5 boson families (this is a stiffness-sharing geometry, agnostic to which boson pairs the flat band).

name: Suhl-Kondo Pairing Amplification (interband scattering manufactures an effective coupling the single band cannot)
basis: Suhl-Kondo interband pair-scattering vertex: even when intraband attraction is weak/zero, a repulsive INTERBAND pair-scattering term g_12 produces a sign-changing (s±) solution of the two-band gap equation with an ENHANCED effective coupling (Kondo, Prog.Theor.Phys.29,1,1963; Mazin et al. on pnictide s±, PRL101,057003,2008). The flat band's huge DOS multiplies the interband channel: the effective coupling lambda_eff ~ g_12 * sqrt(N_flat * N_deep), so a modest g_12 is amplified by the flat-band DOS into a large pairing scale on BOTH bands.
falsifiable_prediction: In the 2-band BdG model, set intraband couplings to zero and only g_12 (interband, repulsive) nonzero. Prediction: a finite s± gap opens on both bands with a pairing scale that grows as sqrt(N_flat) — i.e. flattening the DOS band raises Tc_pairing super-linearly in N_flat at fixed g_12. FALSIFIED if the s± gap on the deep band stays <0.1x the flat-band gap (donor band never inherits enough gap to also be stiff-and-superconducting), OR if the required g_12 to reach 293K pairing exceeds the bandwidth (pushing into the localized/pair-density-wave regime instead of a uniform condensate).
beats_phase_stiffness_ceiling: This attacks the OTHER half of Tc=min(pairing,stiffness): it manufactures a pairing scale large enough that the bottleneck is forced onto the (borrowed) stiffness, and simultaneously puts a real gap on the high-velocity donor band so that band is ITSELF superconducting and ITSELF stiff. A self-superconducting donor band with v_F large has phase stiffness set by its own (large) v_F^2*N_deep, not by spin-fluctuation soft-mode physics — so the Emery-Kivelson cuprate ceiling (a low-D_s, quasi-2D, single-band number) does not apply to it.
novelty: All 5 surveyed boson families ask 'what glues a single band' and hit either a boson-energy ceiling or the stiffness ceiling. Suhl-Kondo amplification is a coupling-MULTIPLICATION mechanism unique to the multiband manifold — there is no single-band analog. It is orthogonal to the boson identity (works for phonon, magnon, or plasmon g_12) and orthogonal to flat-band geometry (uses the flat band only as a DOS reservoir, not for its quantum metric).

name: Leggett-Mode Stiffness Shield (interband phase mode as a fluctuation gap that protects the condensate)
basis: The Leggett mode is a massive (gapped) collective phase oscillation of the relative phase between two band condensates (Leggett 1966; measured in MgB2 Raman, Blumberg et al. PRL99,227002,2007). Because it is GAPPED (mass^2 = 2*g_12*(...)), the relative-phase channel does NOT host the soft thermal phase fluctuations that destroy the cuprate condensate — the dangerous low-energy phase mode is the common (Goldstone/plasmon) mode, while the relative-phase fluctuations are frozen out below the Leggett gap. A two-band condensate thus has FEWER thermally-active phase-destroying modes per unit DOS than a single-band one.
falsifiable_prediction: Compute the finite-T phase-fluctuation correction to T_BKT (or 3D T_c) for the 2-band model with and without the interband g_12 that opens the Leggett gap. Prediction: opening a Leggett gap >> k_B T raises the fluctuation-corrected Tc by suppressing relative-phase fluctuation entropy — a measurable upward shift of Tc vs the g_12=0 baseline at fixed bare stiffness. FALSIFIED if the Leggett gap, once made large enough to matter (g_12 large), simultaneously re-locks the bands into an effective single-band whose stiffness is again min(bands) — i.e. the shield and the donation cannot coexist (a no-free-lunch collapse).
beats_phase_stiffness_ceiling: The 134-164K ceiling is a PHASE-FLUCTUATION ceiling (Emery-Kivelson: order trapped by soft phase modes). If the relative-phase fluctuation channel is gapped out by the Leggett mode, the entropy that drives the BKT/order-melting transition is reduced, so the same bare stiffness supports a HIGHER Tc. This is a route that does not need MORE stiffness — it makes the existing stiffness go further by removing a fluctuation channel that the single-band cuprate cannot remove.
novelty: This inverts the campaign's deep law 'the fluctuation glues, the order traps': here a SECOND condensate's gapped collective mode is used to SHIELD the order from the trapping fluctuation, a lever that literally cannot exist with one band (no relative phase to gap). Orthogonal to every surveyed family, all of which are single-condensate.

name: Velocity-Asymmetric Pair Co-Drag (light donor carries the phase, heavy flat band carries the gap, one condensate)
basis: In a two-band condensate the order parameter is a single coherent object but the supercurrent response is dominated by the LIGHT (high-v_F) band while the gap magnitude is dominated by the HEAVY (flat, high-DOS) band — analogous to the role separation in MgB2 (light/3D σ band sets the robust gap-and-current, π band is the proximitized passenger) but here engineered in REVERSE so the heavy band is the pairing reservoir. Grounded part: superfluid-weight additivity and the v_F-weighting of the Kubo current kernel are textbook two-band BdG. SPECULATIVE part = a host where E_F simultaneously sits in a flat band AND a steep band with strong interband matrix element (e.g. a kagome-derived flat+Dirac pair, CsV3Sb5-class, or a heavy-fermion hybridization gap edge).
falsifiable_prediction: Scan the donor-band Fermi velocity v_F in the 2-band BdG+Kubo model at fixed flat-band pairing. Prediction: T_BKT scales as v_F^2 (linear in the donor-band stiffness) with NO saturation until the donor band depairs (gap closes by velocity-induced kinetic energy). Concretely, a donor band with v_F ~ graphene-scale (1e6 m/s) and N_deep ~ 1 state/eV gives a donated stiffness > 50 meV, predicting T_BKT > 300K at fixed flat-band gap. FALSIFIED if increasing v_F monotonically REDUCES the interband matrix element (faster band decouples from the flat band) so the donated stiffness peaks and falls before reaching the 293K-equivalent — a measurable v_F-vs-coupling trade-off in the band structure.
beats_phase_stiffness_ceiling: Directly: the donated stiffness is a band-velocity stiffness (1/2)N v_F^2, a quantity with NO cuprate-style ceiling — graphene/Dirac velocities give 10-50 meV stiffness scales, well above the 7.4 meV cuprate value. Because the campaign showed flat-band geometric stiffness is intrinsically ~0.1-0.4 meV, the only way to get a large stiffness AND a large DOS at one E_F is to source them from two different bands; the velocity-asymmetric co-drag is the explicit BdG realization of that, and its ceiling is set by real band velocities, not by the spin-fluctuation soft mode.
novelty: Distinct from seed-1 (composite additivity, symmetric) by being a deliberately ASYMMETRIC role assignment with a falsifiable v_F-vs-interband-coupling trade-off as the kill switch — this names the most likely failure mode (decoupling) as the test. Orthogonal to multilayer (no spatial separation, no tunneling loss) and to all boson families (stiffness source is kinetic, not bosonic).

name: Cavity-Bound Defect Polariton Glue (the optical-mode commons)
basis: Each engineered deep-level defect (NV-, SiV-, Er3+-in-host) carries a sharp zero-phonon optical transition. Embed the defect superlattice in a common high-Q photonic/plasmonic cavity (or use a self-formed photonic crystal of the host). The shared cavity photon mediates a long-range, retarded attractive interaction between defect-band carriers via virtual ZPL photon exchange — a cavity-QED Dicke/Tavis-Cummings coupling that becomes a pairing channel. REAL precedents: cavity-mediated electron pairing / cavity-enhanced superconductivity (Sentef, Schlawin, Cavalleri; Schlawin-Cavalleri-Jaksch PRL 2019 'Cavity-Mediated Electron-Photon Superconductivity'; Thomas et al. 2019 vibrational-cavity Tc shift in organics). Defect ZPL ensembles in cavities are REAL (NV-cavity strong coupling, Er3+ silicon-photonics). The defect-band + ZPL-cavity combination as the SC medium is SPECULATIVE.
falsifiable_prediction: In a tight-binding + Tavis-Cummings model of a defect superlattice (filling = half impurity band, ZPL detuning swept), the cavity-mediated pairing vertex Lambda_cav scales as g^2/Delta (collective cooperativity NC) and the induced gap rises super-linearly with defect density n until the ZPL inhomogeneous linewidth exceeds the vacuum Rabi splitting, at which point pairing collapses. Predict a sharp density threshold n* where Tc onset appears, NOT a smooth crossover.
beats_phase_stiffness_ceiling: The Emery-Kivelson ~134-164K ceiling is a phase-stiffness (superfluid weight) wall set by LOCAL spin-fluctuation glue whose stiffness is bounded by exchange J. A cavity photon is delocalized over the whole sample, so the pairing it mediates is intrinsically long-range / infinite-coordination — this directly raises the phase stiffness D_s (which scales with the range and coordination of the pair hopping), decoupling stiffness from the local J that caps the magnon route. The glue is a fluctuation (virtual photon) that never condenses into a competing order, sidestepping 'the order traps'.
novelty: Surveyed plasmon/exciton/magnon glues are all MATTER collective modes with their own competing orders; the cavity photon is a VACUUM/electromagnetic mode external to the electron liquid and carries no charge/spin order to trap. Orthogonal to flat-band geometry (no quantum-geometric tr g needed) and to the 5 boson families. Distinct from H_017 disorder: defects are periodic and the glue is their shared optical mode, not scattering.

name: Hund-Locked Lambda-Doublet Defect Molecule (internal-orbital pairing)
basis: Choose a defect whose deep level is an orbitally-degenerate, Jahn-Teller-active molecular orbital (e.g. NV- triplet 3A2/3E, or a multi-electron color center with a near-degenerate e-doublet). The on-site Hund's coupling + dynamic Jahn-Teller phonon dresses the orbital into a vibronic 'Lambda-doublet' with a built-in negative-U from the JT relaxation energy (Anderson negative-U, REAL: Anderson PRL 1975; dynamic-JT in NV is REAL, Bersuker). An array of identical negative-U defect molecules = a lattice of pre-formed local pairs whose phase orders through impurity-band hopping — a real-space BEC of defect bipolarons.
falsifiable_prediction: An ED/DMFT solve of the impurity-band + local dynamic-JT (E⊗e) Holstein-Hund model predicts a negative effective U whenever the JT stabilization energy E_JT exceeds Hubbard U/2; in that regime the pair susceptibility diverges at a Tc set by the bipolaron bandwidth, and Tc is NON-MONOTONIC in defect spacing (too dense -> direct overlap kills negative-U; too sparse -> bandwidth -> 0). Predict an optimal defect lattice constant a* maximizing Tc.
beats_phase_stiffness_ceiling: Real-space pre-formed pairs are NOT phase-stiffness limited by a glue boson's exchange energy — the stiffness comes from coherent pair hopping in the impurity band (boson tunneling t_B), which is an engineerable lattice parameter, not a fixed J. Because the binding (E_JT, an intra-defect vibronic energy of order 0.1-0.5 eV) and the phase coherence (t_B) are DECOUPLED knobs, one can in principle push the BKT/condensation scale above 164K by raising t_B while keeping pairs bound — escaping the single-energy-scale trap of the magnon route.
novelty: This is bipolaron/negative-U pairing realized in an ENGINEERED defect array rather than a doped crystal, using the defect's own dynamic Jahn-Teller as the local glue — orthogonal to all 5 itinerant-boson families (the glue is intra-molecular, not a lattice collective mode) and to flat-band geometry. Distinct from phonon family: the relevant phonon is a LOCAL vibronic mode of the defect, ceiling-free in the Migdal-Eliashberg <200meV sense because it is anharmonic/local, not a dispersing acoustic branch.

name: Spin-Addressed Pair-Symmetry Selector (optical glue gating)
basis: Defect color centers have optically-addressable, long-lived spin (NV ground-state spin coherence ms-scale at 300K, REAL).
