Use the defect array's spin as a control field: continuous optical spin-polarization (ODMR pumping, REAL) drives the defect-spin reservoir into a SQUEEZED / fully-polarized non-equilibrium steady state that suppresses pair-breaking spin-flip scattering in the impurity band. This is a defect-lattice realization of the abstract 'squeezed spin bath' idea (cf. campaign brainstorm D7) but with a concrete, room-temperature-coherent host.
falsifiable_prediction: In a Bogoliubov-de Gennes + Lindblad steady-state model where defect spins are continuously optically pumped to polarization P, the pair-breaking rate 1/tau_pb falls as (1-P^2) and the effective Tc rises monotonically with pump-induced P, saturating when P->1. Predict a measurable, reversible, LIGHT-ON/LIGHT-OFF Tc shift (delta Tc proportional to absorbed optical power up to a saturation fluence) — a non-equilibrium superconductor that switches off in the dark.
beats_phase_stiffness_ceiling: The 134-164K ceiling is partly set by spin fluctuations that BOTH glue and pair-break. Optically polarizing the defect spin bath removes the pair-breaking channel WITHOUT removing the glue (which can be a separate phonon/cavity channel), so the phase-stiffness-vs-pairbreaking trade-off that pins the Emery-Kivelson scale is broken by an external (optical) entropy export. The pump continuously removes entropy from the spin sector — an active, non-equilibrium escape rather than an equilibrium glue.
novelty: Orthogonal to every surveyed family because it is NOT a glue at all — it is a pair-PROTECTION mechanism (suppressing the decoherence side of the ledger) unique to optically-spin-addressable defects. The magnon/spin-fluctuation family USES spin fluctuations as glue and is capped by them; here spin fluctuations are the enemy and optical pumping silences them. Not flat-band, not Floquet (steady CW, not coherent drive), not topology.

name: Rare-Earth 4f Ultra-Flat Impurity Band (atomic-localization flat band)
basis: Substitute a periodic array of rare-earth ions (Er3+, Nd3+, Yb3+) on a single sublattice of a wide-gap host. The 4f orbitals are core-like and chemically inert, giving an impurity band whose bandwidth is set by tiny direct 4f-4f overlap — naturally meV-scale flat, with built-in optical/spin addressability (REAL: Er3+:Y2SiO5 telecom qubits, the flattest atomic levels known; rare-earth impurity bands are REAL but normally insulating). Engineer the spacing/host so the 4f band crosses E_F and the host conduction electrons provide a Kondo/Anderson-lattice glue. Heavy-fermion superconductivity in rare-earth lattices is REAL (CeCoIn5); doing it in a DESIGNED dilute defect superlattice is SPECULATIVE.
falsifiable_prediction: A periodic Anderson model with f-level localized at engineered rare-earth sites predicts that the superfluid weight D_s of any f-electron pairing is bounded by the f-band width W_f, which is exponentially small in the defect spacing a (W_f ~ exp(-a/xi_4f)). Therefore Tc/D_s will be MAXIMIZED at the minimum spacing before 4f wavefunctions chemically hybridize/quench — predict a hard upper bound Tc < c*W_f and that pushing spacing down to raise W_f eventually destroys the optical addressability (ZPL broadens), giving an explicit, computable a*-vs-Tc Pareto front.
beats_phase_stiffness_ceiling: It probably does NOT beat the ceiling in the naive limit — and that is the honest, valuable prediction: the same flat-band/narrow-band physics that gives strong correlations also caps D_s (W_f is tiny), reproducing the phase-stiffness wall from the OTHER side (geometric flatness). The only escape is quantum-geometric: if the 4f impurity band can be engineered with nonzero Fubini-Study metric (via host-mediated f-d hybridization giving a multiorbital geometric contribution), D_s gains the geometric term that does not vanish with W_f — directly testing whether the CoSn-style geometric lever survives in an atomically-localized defect band.
novelty: Bridges the defect-lattice axis BACK to the flat-band quantum-geometry lever but via ATOMIC 4f localization instead of crystalline destructive interference — a different microscopic origin of flatness, with optical addressability the crystalline flat bands lack. Orthogonal to the boson families (glue is Kondo/Anderson-lattice, a heavy-fermion mechanism not in the surveyed 5). It is the honest falsifier: it predicts the defect route hits the SAME stiffness wall unless geometry is added, sharpening where the real frontier is.

name: Gurzhi Quench Channel — viscous current with no order parameter to disorder
basis: Real: Gurzhi effect (R. N. Gurzhi 1963) — in the momentum-conserving hydrodynamic regime, resistance can DECREASE with increasing T because e-e collisions thermalize momentum into a fluid that flows past boundaries with viscosity-limited (not impurity-limited) dissipation. Confirmed in PdCoO2 (Moll 2016, Science) and graphene (Bandurin 2016). The SPECULATIVE extension: engineer a channel geometry + carrier density where the Gurzhi minimum sits at 293 K, so the dominant momentum-relaxation channel (Umklapp + boundary) is quenched and effective sheet resistance collapses toward the viscous floor.
falsifiable_prediction: In a clean delafossite-class slab (PdCoO2 / PtCoO2 lineage) with channel width W tuned to the 293 K Gurzhi length, the temperature derivative dR/dT will be NEGATIVE across a finite window bracketing 293 K, and the resistance at the minimum will scale as eta(T)/n^2 (kinematic-viscosity over density-squared), NOT as the impurity-limited residual. If dR/dT >= 0 everywhere at 293 K, or R-min tracks residual resistivity, FALSIFIED.
beats_phase_stiffness_ceiling: It does not try to beat it — it makes the ceiling irrelevant. The 134-164 K wall is a property of the SUPERCONDUCTING phase stiffness (Emery-Kivelson): a fluctuating order-parameter phase that proliferates vortices above T_theta. A viscous normal fluid has NO order-parameter phase, so there is nothing to phase-disorder; its coherence is hydrodynamic (local momentum conservation), set by the e-e thermalization length, which GROWS with the right scattering hierarchy rather than collapsing at a stiffness scale.
novelty: Every surveyed family (phonon/exciton/plasmon/magnon glue, flat-band geometry, multilayer D_s, topology, Floquet) presumes a Cooper-pair condensate whose figure of merit is a pairing/stiffness energy. This seed abandons pairing entirely: the merit is kinematic viscosity / Umklapp suppression. Orthogonal because it lives in the conductivity tensor's viscous sector, not the gap equation.

name: Odd-Viscosity Edge Lane — chiral dissipationless boundary current from Hall viscosity
basis: Real: Hall (odd) viscosity is a genuinely DISSIPATIONLESS transport coefficient (Avron-Seiler-Zograf 1995; measured in graphene by Berdyugin et al., Science 2019, science.aau0685). In a T-broken hydrodynamic fluid the viscous stress tensor has an antisymmetric eta_H component that does NO work. SPECULATIVE extension: in a fluid where odd viscosity dominates the even (dissipative) viscosity (eta_H >> eta), a self-organized chiral edge LANE forms in which momentum flux is carried by the dissipationless component, giving an anomalously low longitudinal resistance distinct from both the quantum Hall edge (single-particle) and SC.
falsifiable_prediction: In a hydrodynamic 2D fluid under a weak (non-quantizing) B with engineered Berry-curvature / mass-asymmetry to maximize eta_H/eta, the longitudinal sheet resistance of a Hall-bar edge channel will fall BELOW the bulk viscous-flow value by a factor set by (eta_H/eta)^2, and will show the eta_H sign-reversal under B-flip while the bulk does not. If edge R = bulk R, or no eta_H/eta enhancement of conductance, FALSIFIED.
beats_phase_stiffness_ceiling: The dissipationless channel is eta_H, a single Berry-phase/geometric coefficient of the NORMAL fluid — it has no Tc and no order-parameter phase, so the Emery-Kivelson stiffness collapse cannot apply. Its temperature ceiling is instead set by where the fluid exits the hydrodynamic regime (e-e rate falls below e-phonon + boundary rate), which is a kinematic crossover that can in principle be pushed above 293 K by raising carrier density and Debye-decoupling, not a thermodynamic stiffness wall.
novelty: Topology/fractionalization was surveyed as an INDEPENDENT lever and closed-negative — but that was single-particle band topology and anyon sectors. Odd viscosity is a COLLECTIVE, hydrodynamic geometric response (curvature of the many-body stress tensor), not a band Chern number; it survives in a gapless interacting fluid where band topology gives nothing. Distinct lever.

name: Second-Sound Supercurrent — coherent collective hydrodynamic mode as the carrier
basis: Real: hydrodynamic electron fluids support a SOUND-LIKE collective mode (electron 'second sound' / demon-like in-phase or out-of-phase density oscillation) — predicted for graphene (arXiv:2011.03806) and the tomographic-Fermi-liquid collective modes (arXiv:2208.13797); second sound is a weakly-damped energy/entropy wave in the momentum-conserving regime. SPECULATIVE extension: drive current through the propagating, weakly-damped second-sound channel rather than the diffusive quasiparticle channel — a coherent collective oscillation carries the charge/energy with damping set by the (small) even viscosity, not by single-particle scattering.
falsifiable_prediction: An AC current injected at the second-sound resonance frequency omega_ss(T) of a clean hydrodynamic strip will show a sharp conductance PEAK (collective-mode resonance) whose quality factor Q = omega_ss / (damping ~ eta k^2) exceeds the DC drift-conductance figure of merit, and Q survives to 293 K in materials with e-e rate >> e-phonon rate. If no resonant conductance enhancement appears, or Q collapses well below 293 K tracking the e-phonon rate, FALSIFIED.
beats_phase_stiffness_ceiling: A second-sound mode is a NORMAL-state collective excitation, not a broken-symmetry condensate — it has no superfluid phase rigidity and therefore no phase-stiffness ceiling. Its coherence is set by mode damping eta*k^2, which can be made small at fixed T by working at long wavelength / high e-e rate. The 134-164 K wall (a vortex-proliferation / phase-fluctuation scale of an SC order parameter) has no analog here; the relevant high-T cutoff is the hydrodynamic-regime exit, a kinematic and tunable boundary.
novelty: Plasmon glue was surveyed (no clean >=349 meV mode to GLUE pairs). This seed does NOT use the collective mode as glue for Cooper pairs — it uses the collective mode ITSELF as the charge carrier, removing pairing from the loop. Orthogonal: plasmon-as-glue vs second-sound-as-conductor.

name: Viscosity-Floor Inversion — push the Planckian viscosity minimum into a coherence maximum
basis: Real: the Planckian bound eta/s >~ hbar/(4 pi k_B) (KSS-style lower bound on shear-viscosity-to-entropy ratio, Kovtun-Son-Starinets 2005, applied to electron fluids in WP2, arXiv:1706.05925) means the strongest-correlated electron fluids sit near a MINIMUM viscosity. SPECULATIVE inversion: in a Hall-bar geometry the MEASURED resistance from viscous flow scales as eta/n^2, so a fluid simultaneously at the Planckian eta-floor AND high density n minimizes viscous resistance — a 'most-perfect-fluid' conductance maximum, the hydrodynamic dual of a high-Tc condensate but with no condensate.
falsifiable_prediction: Across a family of correlated metals, the viscous-flow resistance at 293 K will be a monotone function of (eta/s)*(s/n^2); materials approaching the Planckian floor eta/s ~ hbar/4pi k_B at high n will show the lowest viscous resistivity, independent of any pairing scale. If viscous resistivity does NOT correlate with proximity to the Planckian eta/s floor, or correlates instead with a pairing gap, FALSIFIED.
beats_phase_stiffness_ceiling: The figure of merit is eta/s vs the KSS bound — a NORMAL-fluid quantity with no order parameter and hence no phase stiffness to cap. The Planckian floor is a LOWER bound on dissipation that the best fluids approach but it carries no intrinsic temperature scale at which coherence dies (unlike the Emery-Kivelson stiffness), so 293 K is not privileged; the limiting T is again the hydrodynamic-regime exit, set by the e-e vs e-phonon rate hierarchy.
novelty: Demand-relaxation and the various stiffness-ceiling levers were all framed around a Tc / D_s of a condensate. This seed reframes the entire merit function as eta/s proximity to the holographic Planckian bound — a transport-thermodynamic ratio orthogonal to every pairing-energy and quantum-geometry lever surveyed. It also imports a genuinely different theoretical apparatus (gauge-gravity viscosity bound) absent from the campaign.

name: Critically-Damped Phason Bath (the Goldilocks-Damping Glue)
basis: Setty, Ghosh, Phillips et al., 'Glassy heat capacity from overdamped phasons and a hypothetical phason-induced superconductivity in incommensurate structures', PRB 108, 054203 (2023) / arXiv:2305.05407. They show the phason's wavevector-INDEPENDENT damping Gamma (the anomalous feature distinguishing phasons from acoustic phonons) strongly enhances the effective Eliashberg coupling lambda, and Tc has a sharp NON-MONOTONIC maximum exactly at the underdamped->overdamped crossover Gamma ~ Omega_phason. The pairing boson is the structural phason of an incommensurate host (composite/host-guest crystal), NOT a phonon, exciton, plasmon, or magnon.
falsifiable_prediction: In an incommensurate composite crystal (e.g. a host-guest elemental high-pressure phase, or Sr14Cu24O41-class composite), self-consistent Migdal-Eliashberg with a damped-phason kernel D(q,omega) = Omega^2/(omega^2 + i*Gamma*omega - Omega^2) predicts Tc(Gamma) that RISES then FALLS, peaking at Gamma* ~ Omega_phason; sweeping pinning frequency Omega via a commensurability/impurity knob must move the Tc maximum monotonically with Omega*. If Tc is flat or monotonic in Gamma, the mechanism is falsified.
beats_phase_stiffness_ceiling: The 134-164K wall is the Emery-Kivelson phase-stiffness ceiling of a MAGNETIC (spin-fluctuation) Goldstone, set by the electronic superfluid density. The phason is a STRUCTURAL Goldstone of the host lattice with its own stiffness (host elastic modulus) and a damping-controlled coupling that is parametrically decoupled from the electronic D_s. lambda is enhanced by damping rather than by softening a magnetic stiffness, so the binding scale is set by Omega_phason and Gamma, not by spin stiffness — the spin-fluctuation ceiling simply does not apply to a lattice Goldstone.
novelty: Surveyed phonon glue used acoustic/optical branches (<=200meV ceiling) protected by translational symmetry; the phason is explicitly NOT protected, carries q-independent damping, and its GLUE STRENGTH GROWS with damping — the opposite sign of the usual 'damping kills coherence' intuition. Orthogonal to magnon (magnetic stiffness ceiling), exciton (order-trap), plasmon, and to the electronic-Goldstone Tkachenko/localizer seeds which used an ELECTRONIC mode, not a structural one.

name: Discommensuration-Lattice Emergent Boson (the Soliton-Mesh Glue)
basis: Chen, Yu, Phillips et al., 'Discommensuration-driven/enhanced superconductivity in the CDW phases of TMDs', PRB 99, 121108(R) (2019) / arXiv:1806.08064. In the NEARLY-commensurate regime an incommensurate state breaks into commensurate domains separated by discommensurations (a 2D soliton/domain-wall mesh); the phason becomes GAPPED on the discommensuration superlattice and the emergent soliton lattice mediates an attractive electron-electron interaction. The glue boson is the collective sliding/breathing mode of the discommensuration MESH, distinct from a bulk phason or a phonon.
falsifiable_prediction: Tuning a host across the commensurate->nearly-commensurate->incommensurate sequence (pressure or doping), Tc must peak in the NEARLY-commensurate window where discommensuration density is finite but the mesh is still rigid, and the pairing vertex must scale with discommensuration areal density n_DC; a momentum-resolved gap on STM/ARPES should track the discommensuration superlattice vectors, not the atomic lattice. If Tc is maximal in the fully-incommensurate (mesh-melted) limit instead, falsified.
beats_phase_stiffness_ceiling: Pairing strength is set by the discommensuration mesh stiffness and density (a geometric/structural quantity tunable by commensurability), not by the electronic spin stiffness. Because the emergent boson lives on a long-wavelength superlattice, its characteristic energy and the resulting phase stiffness of the SC condensate are renormalized by the mesh elastic constants — a separate, lattice-derived stiffness budget that can exceed the magnetic 134-164K ceiling without invoking spin fluctuations.
novelty: Distinct from 1T-TiSe2 (where the exciton IS the CDW and the order traps): here the CDW is already commensurate-locked and SUPERCONDUCTIVITY rides on its DEFECT lattice (discommensurations), so the order is not competing — the topological defects of the order are the glue. Orthogonal to all 5 surveyed boson families and to the flat-band/topology levers; the carrier is an emergent soliton-lattice mode, a structural-defect collective, not a clean Goldstone.

name: Host-Guest Sliding-Chain Boson (the Incommensurate-Rail Glue)
basis: Incommensurate host-guest structures (compressed elemental S, Bi, Sc, Ba; Degtyareva et al.; Sr14Cu24O41 composite) possess quasi-acoustic 'sliding modes' where the guest sublattice slides almost freely against the host — a phason of the composite crystal. arXiv:2107.04397 (compressed sulfur host-guest SC) and PMC5898833 ('Strong coupling superconductivity in a quasiperiodic host-guest structure') report strong-coupling SC where these extra sliding/sliding-phason branches add to the pairing spectrum. The boson is the inter-sublattice sliding phason, a genuinely extra low-energy structural branch absent in any commensurate crystal.
falsifiable_prediction: In a host-guest superconductor, the Eliashberg alpha^2 F(omega) must contain a distinct low-frequency peak from the guest-chain sliding mode below the acoustic host phonons; isotopically substituting the GUEST sublattice only (leaving host fixed) should shift Tc with a guest-mass isotope exponent that the host-only phonon picture cannot produce. If the guest-only isotope effect is zero, the sliding-phason contribution is falsified.
beats_phase_stiffness_ceiling: The sliding phason adds a SECOND, lower-energy boson branch ON TOP of the conventional phonon spectrum, increasing total lambda = lambda_phonon + lambda_phason at fixed electronic stiffness — a parallel (additive) glue channel rather than a competitor. Because it is structural, it sidesteps the spin-fluctuation stiffness budget entirely; the combined lambda can push the conventional (phonon-bound) Tc ceiling upward without ever touching the magnetic 134-164K wall.
novelty: The surveyed phonon family capped at ~200meV optical phonons of a SINGLE commensurate lattice; host-guest composites have an EXTRA quasi-acoustic branch with no commensurate analogue and anomalously weak restoring force (near-free sliding), so it is a different lattice DOF, not just a softer phonon. Orthogonal to magnon/plasmon/exciton and to flat-band geometry; this is an additive structural channel unique to incommensurate composites.

name: Pinning-Memory Ratchet (the Glassy-Phason Non-Equilibrium Stiffness Ballast)
basis: SPECULATIVE extension of two real results: (1) overdamped phasons give a glass-like LINEAR-in-T specific heat and a manifold of localized two-level systems in the shallow corrugated phason phase-space (Cano & Levanyuk; Setty et al. arXiv:2305.05407), and (2) pinned phasons carry hysteretic memory / threshold-field depinning (CDW sliding). The SPECULATION: use the pinned-phason glass as a STIFFNESS BALLAST — a reservoir of slow structural modes that, when the SC condensate phase tries to fluctuate, are dragged adiabatically and add inertia (phase mass) to the SC order parameter, raising the effective phase stiffness rather than providing pairing glue. No paper claims this; the glue->ballast repurposing is invented.
falsifiable_prediction: If a pinned-phason glass ballasts the SC phase, then the superfluid-stiffness onset T_theta (Nernst/diamagnetic fluctuation onset) should DECOUPLE from and exceed the pairing-gap onset, and should depend on phason PINNING strength (impurity/commensurability) — purer samples (lower pinning, freer phason) give LOWER stiffness, the opposite of normal disorder behavior. If stiffness tracks the gap or improves with purity, falsified.
beats_phase_stiffness_ceiling: The Emery-Kivelson ceiling is a statement about the ELECTRONIC superfluid density setting phase stiffness. If a slow structural glass bath couples to the SC phase and contributes its own inertia/stiffness term to the phase action (rho_s,total = rho_s,electronic + rho_s,phason-glass), the total stiffness is no longer bounded by the spin-fluctuation budget alone — the lattice glass donates extra stiffness, lifting T_theta above 134-164K even though the pairing scale is unchanged.
novelty: Inverts the campaign's deep law ('the FLUCTUATION glues, the ORDER traps'): here a near-FROZEN (pinned, glassy) structural mode neither glues nor traps but BALLASTS — a third role for a lattice DOF. Distinct from the multilayer D_s boost (which adds electronic layers) and from demand-relaxation; the stiffness donor is a non-electronic, non-equilibrium structural glass. Orthogonal to every surveyed family because it targets the STIFFNESS wall directly rather than the pairing channel.

name: Janus-Orbital Stiffness Delegation (the pairing orbital and the stiffness orbital are different)
basis: GROUNDED core, SPECULATIVE composition. Real: in a Hund's metal the orbitals split into incoherent/local (heavy, low Z, near half-filling) and coherent/itinerant (light, high Z) sub-bands — Werner-Millis spin-freezing (PRL 101.166405) and the FeSe orbital-selective Cooper pairing of Sprau/Davis (Nature Mater. s41563-018-0151-0, Z_xy < Z_xz << Z_yz). The SF/magnon ceiling exists because ONE channel must both pair and carry stiffness (Emery-Kivelson). Speculative lever: deliberately DELEGATE — let the incoherent orbital supply the pairing amplitude via local Hund-locked moment fluctuations, and let the coherent orbital (with its OWN large Drude weight / superfluid stiffness, set by its bandwidth, not by the pairing scale) carry D_s, the two coupled only by inter-orbital pair-hopping ~J.
falsifiable_prediction: In a 2-orbital DMFT/ED Hund model tuned to the OSMT boundary, the computed superfluid stiffness D_s will track the COHERENT orbital's renormalized Drude weight (Z_coh * bare D) and become DECOUPLED from the pairing gap Delta_inc of the incoherent orbital — i.e. D_s/Delta does NOT collapse to the Emery-Kivelson O(1) ratio but exceeds it, with D_s set by Z_coh*W_coh >> Delta_inc. If D_s and Delta remain locked (ratio pinned at the single-channel value), the seed is falsified.
beats_phase_stiffness_ceiling: The ~134-164K wall is a SINGLE-channel tradeoff: the magnon/SF that glues also softens the phase. Here pairing energy lives on the incoherent orbital while stiffness lives on a SEPARATE coherent orbital whose D_s is fixed by its own bare bandwidth W_coh (eV-scale), not by the pairing boson. T_c is then bounded by min(Delta-driven mean-field, separate large D_s) rather than by the coupled product, structurally lifting the stiffness ceiling above ~164K.
novelty: Surveyed magnon/SF family treats pairing and stiffness as one renormalized fluid hitting the Emery-Kivelson wall. This seed makes glue = on-site Hund J (not a propagating boson) and physically SEPARATES the pairing carrier from the stiffness carrier across two orbitals — a degree of freedom the boson families and flat-band/multilayer D_s-boost levers do not have (those still co-locate pairing and stiffness).

name: Spin-Freezing Critical Glue at the OSMT Knee (sqrt-omega self-energy as the pairing kernel)
basis: GROUNDED. Werner-Millis: at the spin-freezing crossover the self-energy goes as Im Sigma ~ sqrt(omega) over a wide range — a marginal/non-Fermi-liquid kernel with NO single boson energy scale (PRL 101.166405; arXiv:1207.3033; spin-freezing & Sachdev-Ye link arXiv:1805.04102). Werner et al. note local-moment fluctuations at this knee can induce pairing as a generic unconventional-SC mechanism. Speculative lever: site the material exactly on the Hund-driven OSMT spin-freezing line so the glue is the BROADBAND sqrt(omega) Hund-moment kernel rather than a peaked magnon — a flat, gapless, J-set spectrum with effective cutoff ~ J (hundreds of meV) instead of the ~50-100 meV magnon zone-boundary.
falsifiable_prediction: Eliashberg inversion on the DMFT-computed Hund-moment susceptibility chi(omega) at the spin-freezing line will yield an effective glue spectral function with weight extending to ~J (>=300 meV) and a frequency-integrated coupling lambda_eff that, fed into a McMillan/Allen-Dynes estimate, gives a mean-field T_c ceiling >200K — strictly higher than the magnon-zone-boundary cutoff gives for the same lambda. If the inverted kernel's spectral weight is confined below the magnon ~100 meV scale, falsified.
beats_phase_stiffness_ceiling: The 134-164K wall is set by the magnon BANDWIDTH/zone-boundary that simultaneously caps glue cutoff and erodes stiffness. The sqrt(omega) Hund kernel is gapless AND extends to J ~ several hundred meV, so the glue's high-frequency weight (which sets the pairing scale without proportionally killing stiffness, because it is local on-site J not an itinerant spin texture) raises the attainable Delta while leaving the coherent-channel stiffness largely intact. The wall moves because the glue cutoff is J, not the magnon zone boundary.
novelty: The surveyed magnon/spin-fluctuation family uses a PEAKED, bandwidth-limited propagating boson and hits the Emery-Kivelson wall. This uses the BROADBAND, scale-free critical self-energy of the Hund spin-freezing QCP as the kernel — a fundamentally different (gapless, local, J-cutoff) glue spectrum not in the 5-boson list, and distinct from negative-U which is static.

name: Negative-Effective-Hund Pair Channel a la Jahn-Teller Metal (A3C60 inverted-J, run to ambient via geometry)
basis: GROUNDED anchor, SPECULATIVE target. Real: alkali fullerides A3C60 superconduct next to a Mott insulator via an EFFECTIVELY NEGATIVE Hund coupling produced when dynamic Jahn-Teller phonons over-screen J, giving spontaneous orbital-selective Mott transitions and the 'Jahn-Teller metal' (Capone/Fabrizio; PRL 118.177002; arXiv:1609.00136). The pairing here is a LOCAL on-molecule intra-orbital singlet stabilized by inverted J — orthogonal to spin fluctuations. Speculative lever: replicate the inverted-J local-pair channel in a designed multi-orbital lattice (not C60) where the JT-overscreening sits in an incoherent orbital while a coherent orbital supplies stiffness, pushing the A3C60 ~38K toward ambient by enlarging the coherent-orbital bandwidth.
falsifiable_prediction: In a 3-orbital Hund-Hubbard model with a tunable effective J, the s-wave local-pair susceptibility will DIVERGE only for J_eff < 0 (inverted) and the resulting T_c will scale with the coherent-orbital bandwidth W_coh, NOT with |J_eff|. Prediction: increasing W_coh at fixed J_eff<0 raises T_c monotonically with no stiffness collapse, breaking the W-independent fulleride scaling. If T_c saturates/falls as W_coh grows (stiffness collapse), falsified.
beats_phase_stiffness_ceiling: Inverted-J pairing is a LOCAL on-site singlet — its pairing scale is set by |J_eff| and is NOT a soft propagating mode, so it does not carry the magnon's stiffness-eroding low-energy spectral weight. Phase stiffness is supplied separately by the coherent orbital's bandwidth, decoupled from the local pair binding. The Emery-Kivelson product ceiling does not apply because glue (local J) and stiffness (band W) are independent knobs.
novelty: The surveyed negative-U family is a STATIC bipolaronic/charge attraction. Inverted-Hund is dynamically generated by JT-overscreening of the SPIN/ORBITAL exchange and is intrinsically multi-orbital + orbital-selective — a distinct microscopic origin (J-channel, not U-channel) and distinct from the exciton/plasmon/phonon boson list. Not the magnon family (no spin-fluctuation propagator).

name: Z-Quench Resonance: tuning quasiparticle weight Z_inc -> 0 to make the incoherent orbital an all-pairing, zero-Drude reservoir
basis: SPECULATIVE, built on grounded ingredients. Real: at OSMT the incoherent orbital's quasiparticle weight Z collapses toward 0 while the coherent orbital keeps Z~O(1) (orbital-selective Mottness, FeSe Z-hierarchy, arXiv:2506.16384; two-orbital attractive-Hubbard+Hund SC-insulator transition arXiv:2505.08597). A Z->0 orbital contributes ~0 to Drude weight / superfluid stiffness (D_s ~ Z) but its LOCAL pair amplitude (set by atomic Hund/Coulomb, not by Z) stays finite. Speculative claim: at the exact Z-quench point the incoherent orbital becomes a pure pairing reservoir (all binding, no stiffness burden) injecting pairs into the coherent orbital by Andreev-like inter-orbital pair-hopping ~J.
falsifiable_prediction: Sweeping toward the OSMT in 2-orbital DMFT/ED, the incoherent orbital's contribution to D_s will vanish as Z_inc while its anomalous (pair) amplitude F_inc stays finite; total D_s remains ~ Z_coh*W_coh, so the maximum T_c (bounded by min(Delta, D_s)) is set ENTIRELY by the coherent orbital and is INDEPENDENT of Z_inc. Concrete: T_c(D_s-limited) plotted vs Z_inc is flat. If D_s tracks Z_inc downward (incoherent orbital still taxes stiffness), falsified.
beats_phase_stiffness_ceiling: Emery-Kivelson binds T_c because the glue-carrying fluctuations also dress and soften the SAME carriers that must stay phase-stiff. Here the pairing reservoir has Z_inc->0, so by D_s ~ Z it carries essentially ZERO stiffness burden — it cannot erode phase rigidity because it has none to begin with. Stiffness is fully outsourced to the high-Z coherent orbital. The ceiling is the coherent orbital's Z_coh*W_coh, which can be eV-scale (>>164K) by material choice.
novelty: Orthogonal to all 5 boson families (no propagating glue at all — pair transfer is via local J pair-hopping) and to flat-band quantum-geometry / multilayer D_s-boost (those raise stiffness within ONE fluid near a band-touching; this instead REMOVES the pairing reservoir from the stiffness budget via Z-quenching). It exploits orbital-selective Mottness as a stiffness-isolation trick, a lever absent from the surveyed list.
